title: The One With Monica’s Thunder

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what’s going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I found a note on my door, "Come to Monica’s quick, bring champagne and a Three Musketeers bar."

lines.text: Yeah I’ll take that.

lines.text: I’m engaged!!!!!! I’m engaged!!!!

lines.text: Yeah, she’s been out there for twenty minutes, I’m surprised you didn’t hear her on the way over.

lines.text: Oh, I thought it was just a kid yelling, "I’m gay! I’m gay!" Can I bring her in?

lines.text: I’m getting married!!!! I’m gonna be a bride!!!! No, I will not shut up because I’m engaged! Ohh, big talk! Huh, why don’t you come over here and say that to me?! Huh, buddy?! Yeah, my fiancee will kick your ass! Come on, apartment 20! Apartment 20!
lines.stage_direction: Someone else yells at her. He yells again. Chandler starts to look worried.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, you get her in here. You bolt the door. I’ll be in the closet.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe are standing around the table drinking champagne as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! Wait-wait-wait! Shhh! Okay, umm, I just wanna say that…I love you guys so-so much and-and thank you for being here on my special night. Our special night. I mean it just wouldn’t be my—our-our night, if you all weren’t here to celebrate with me—us—Damnit!
lines.stage_direction: Bangs on her class with a spoon to make a toast. Chandler clears his throat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s okay, I want this to be your night too. To Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Awww, come on—wait—stop it. Okay, to Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To Monica!

lines.text: So have you decided on a band for the wedding? Because, y’know, I’m kinda musical.

lines.text: Okay everybody, let’s go! Let’s go!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

He’s got the wool cap, he’s got the cut-off Knicks jersey over the faded T-shirt, and he’s got the whole pants-around-the-knees-showing-off-the-boxers thing that rich, white, suburban kids have adopted in a desperate and extremely futile attempt to try to look like they’re from the inner-city.

lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, I am so…so happy for you guys, but you getting married just reminds me of the fact that I’m not. I’m not even close. And I don’t know, maybe I just wanted to make myself feel better. And I know that that’s dumb, but oh my God you were so depressed when Ross got married that you slept with Chandler!

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t care, she slept with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anyway sweetie, I am, I’m so sorry I ruined your night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m sorry I almost made you sleep with Ross.

scenes.scene_description: Ross looks at Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m going to take off. Congratulations man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

scenes.scene_description: They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What can I say, you missed your chance. From now on the only person who’s going to enjoy these bad boys is me.
lines.stage_direction: holds up his hands Quickly realizes what he said and exits disgustedly.

scenes.scene_description: They hug

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Here’s a dollar, consider it a deposit. Please sing at our wedding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now… " Who will perform the ceremony! Who will perform the cer ——Oh—oh! All right, I’ll pound on him in the morning.
lines.stage_direction: Starts singing again Chandler enters and grabs her guitar and closes the door behind him

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Phoebe is singing outside Monica and Chandler’s door.

title: The One With Rachel’s Book

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who wants French toast?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I’ll have some!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good, me too. Eggs and milk are in the fridge. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oww!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What’s the matter honey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know, my hand feels weird. I guess it’s because, I’m engaged! How long before it starts getting annoying?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It starts?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, so let’s get started on the wedding plans!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Already?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we got a lot to do! We gotta think about the flowers, the caterers, the music…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I got some thoughts on that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait Chandler, too many cooks…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Take from me, as the groom all you have to do is show up and try to say the right name.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay!
lines.stage_direction: returning Sets down a huge 3" 3-ring binder on the table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What in God’s name is that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God, the wedding book?! I haven’t seen that since the forth grade!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This baby has got everything. Take y’know, locations for instance. First, organized alphabetically, then geographically, then by square footage.
lines.stage_direction: She opens up the binder to the locations chapter.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That is so smart! Break it off. Break it off now.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, everyone is there having breakfast and Joey enters carrying a loaf of bread.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that should conclusively prove that I had the idea for Jurassic Park first! Now let’s take a look at…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, oh my God! Wh-wh-what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I need to talk to you, it’s pretty urgent. It’s about Monica and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Of course, of course. Umm, would you please excuse me for a moment? Umm, do you know each other’s hometowns? Why don’t you… Wh-what’s going on?
lines.stage_direction: To the class. Motions that they should learn everyone’s hometown. To Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, umm, not much. But, I was just thinking that since those guys just got engaged that maybe it would be nice if they had some privacy, y’know? So, could I just move in with you for a couple days?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, okay, yeah, sure. But wh-what’s wrong with Monica and Chandler?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing—Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, you said it was urgent!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah it is! I’m going to the movies and it starts in like five minutes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you realize I have a classroom full of students?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry. I’m so rude. Does anyone want to come to the movies?

scenes.scene_description: A Classroom, Ross is giving a lecture.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, so I haven’t cleared the budget with my parents yet, but tell me how this is for music.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right umm, a string quartet for the procession.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Aw.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A jazz trio for cocktails. The Bay City Rollers for dancing. Wait, that was from my sixth grade wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you couldn’t get them anyway. Ian doesn’t plan anymore and Derrick… And Derrick is a name I shouldn’t know.
lines.stage_direction: Off of Rachel and Monica’s looks

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Mon, do you have another pillow? Y’know, something a little snugglyer?
lines.stage_direction: sitting up from the couch Holds up one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why are you napping over here instead of over at your place?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, the duck…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! The duck?! What the hell did the damn duck do now?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, well he did not get sick somewhere in there and it was immediately found and properly cleaned up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, do I get to look at this book or is it just for people who are actually involved in the wedding?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course you can look at it! Yeah, I want your opinion too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here you go! What do you think about centerpieces?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Centerpieces!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Roses or Lilies?
lines.stage_direction: Holds up a picture of each.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Definitely roses. Well, I just think they’re a little more weddingy. But Lilies are the clear choice.
lines.stage_direction: Monica and Rachel exchange a look. Monica holds the Lily picture closer to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! It’s like one mind.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh-huh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guys! Guys!! You gotta let me nap! Ugh, I’m gonna get cranky!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, there is a perfectly good couch across the hall!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes it is perfectly good, and it is not one of the places the duck got sick!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I’m gonna go!
lines.stage_direction: Gets up and heads for the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now Joey, what did the duck do?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know! But he did not eat your face cream!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey little buddy, how are you feeling? What the hell is in that face cream? That’s so soft. Pillowcases!
lines.stage_direction: The duck does not get sick and Joey recoils in horror and heads for the couch. He’s about to try out the couch but notices the bed in Rachel’s room. He walks into her room and feels the bed. He pulls back the comforter. He climbs in and groans in delight. Suddenly, he feels something under him and pulls out a little beat up paperback book. He opens it and starts to read from it.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Rachel and Monica are pretty much telling Chandler what the wedding plans are.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Phoebe…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Ross, hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m sorry, I’m with a client right now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, let’s talk outside.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s apartment, Phoebe has moved in and has a massage client on her table she’s set up in the living room. Ross enters and is shocked to see a naked man lying on the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, you can’t massage people in my apartment!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What’s the big deal? I did it at Monica and Chandler’s!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And they knew about it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well Ross, what is this really about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, this is my home and I want to be able to come and go whenever I want!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I will find someplace else to do the rest of my appointments. I just don’t know what the big deal is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The big deal is I don’t want naked, greasy strangers in my apartment when I want to kick back with a puzzle—beer! Cold beer.

scenes.scene_description: They go into the hall.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, what ‘cha doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sweepin’. Why? Turn you on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. What if I was sweeping a chimney?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, did you my face cream?

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sweeping up as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where are you going? The vicar won’t be home for hours.

scenes.scene_description: She walks into her bedroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, where did you learn that word?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where do you think, Zelda?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You found my book?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah I did!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, what-what are you doing going into my bedroom?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, look I’m sorry, I went in there to take a nap and I know I shouldn’t have, but you got porn!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey-hey, y’know what? I don’t care! I’m not ashamed of my book. There’s nothing with a woman enjoying a little…erotica. It’s just a healthy expression of female sexuality, which by the way, you will never understand.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got porn!

scenes.scene_description: She comes back out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, is uh Phoebe here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh no-no, she-she’s out for the night.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Ohh great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can I, can I help you with something?

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Well, I don’t know. Are you a masseur?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes I am.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Great! Dad! Thank you so much, I’ll be back to pick him up in an hour.
lines.stage_direction: Calls down the hall Her old father walks in. She walks away.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, Ross is coming out of the living room carrying his salad and a puzzle—beer! Cold beer. And he decides to fold up Phoebe’s massage table, but being Ross has trouble with it as there is a knock on the door. He sets the table back up and opens the door to reveal a beautiful woman.
Ross isn’t happy and closes the door slowly.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: So Chandler, you’re parents must’ve been thrilled when you told them you were engaged.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, I should probably call them.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I remember when we first got engaged.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don’t think I ever heard that story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh dad, really you don’t need to…

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, I’d gotten Judy pregnant. I still don’t know that happened.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You don’t know how that happened?! Your dog thought my diaphragm was a chew toy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a sweet story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, at least you’re not hearing it for the first time at your fifth grade Halloween party.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: What?! They wanted a scary story!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anyway, we’re really excited about our wedding plans, and well I guess pretty soon we’ll be making a big withdrawal from the Monica wedding fund. What?
lines.stage_direction: Chandler and her laugh, but her parents don’t.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You tell her Jack, I can’t do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened? You still have the Monica wedding fund don’t you?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: We have it. Only now, we call it the beach house.

scenes.scene_description: A Restaurant, Monica and Chandler are having dinner with her parents.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t believe you spent my wedding fund on the beach house!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We’re sorry honey, but we just assumed if you got married after you turned 30 you’d pay for it yourself.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bought the beach house when I was 23!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Which means you had seven years of beach fun and you can’t put a price on that sweetie.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We really do feel bad about this though.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: We started saving again when you were dating Richard and then that went to hell, so we redid the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about when I started dating Chandler?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Well it was Chandler! We didn’t think he’d ever propose!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Clearly I did not start drinking enough at the start of the meal.

scenes.scene_description: A Restaurant, scene continued from before the break.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t believe it! That there is no money for my wedding?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: We might still have some money, if your father didn’t think it was a good idea to sell ice over the Internet.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: It seemed like such a simple idea.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Stupid Jack, the word is stupid.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: All right, enough! I don’t want to hear about it anymore! Good luck, Chandler.
lines.stage_direction: Under his breath Chandler takes another drink.

scenes.scene_description: Starts to make up for lost time and takes a big swig of his drink.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! Now, I’m going to touch you. Ohh, that’s soft.
lines.stage_direction: He does so, very gingerly. He starts poking him and notices his salad spoons and starts to massage him with those.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, his massage client is on the table and Ross is reluctantly starting his massage. He spreads some lotion in his hands, and doesn’t like it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t believe this. Do you think that your parents could help pay for it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know, my mother spent most of her money on her fourth wedding. She’s saving the rest for her divorce. And any extra cash my father has he saves for his yearly trips to Dollywood .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what happened at dinner?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My parents spent the money for our wedding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My God! What did you order?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, but there’s no money! Well this is terrible! You guys are gonna have to get married in like a, rec. center!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, it’s gonna be okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No it’s not! It’s not gonna be okay! It sucks! No swing band! No lilies!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, y’know what? It’s gonna be okay. I mean you don’t have to have this rustic Italian feast. Y’know? And-and you don’t need, you don’t need this custom-made, empire waisted, duchess, satin gown; you can wear off the rack.
lines.stage_direction: She starts to cry, as does Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, it really is gonna be okay. The important thing is that we love each other and that we’re gonna get married.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you even understand what off the rack means?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, why don’t you just pay for it yourself?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How? I don’t have any money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have some.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, close to…
lines.stage_direction: Notices Rachel leaning in to hear and decides to write it on a piece of paper and hand it to Monica as Phoebe averts her eyes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa!!! Are you kidding me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well what?! How-how much is it?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s enough for wedding scenario eight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How great are you, you little saver?! I mean, the-the amount you have is exactly the budget of my dream wedding!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, you guys are so made for each other.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you’re not suggesting that we spend all of the money on the wedding?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, come on, I’ve been saving this money for six years and I kinda had some of it earmarked for the future, not just for a party.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Hello, Mr. Chandler.
lines.stage_direction: reading the slip of paper In a sultry voice

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is the most special day of our lives.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I realize that honey, but I’m not gonna spend all of the money on one party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, umm I-I love you, but umm, if you call our wedding a party one more time, you may not get invited. Okay? Listen, we could always earn more money, okay? But uh, we’re only gonna get married once.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I understand, but I have to put my foot down. Okay? The answer is no.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You-you’re gonna have to put your foot down?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I am!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, money and a firm hand. Finally a Chandler I can get on board with.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Chandler are returning from dinner, Rachel is already there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach, do you smell smoke?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh, I get it, smoke, chimney, chimney sweep, very funny, ha-ha.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, I’m serious. You don’t smell it? Something’s on fire.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well no, I don’t smell anything.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y’know what? It’s probably just your burnin’ loins.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what are you guys, what are you guys talking about?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damn, this coffee’s cold! Hey Rach, do you mind if I heat this up on your loins?
lines.stage_direction: takes a drink Joey and he both laugh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, I can not believe you told him, Joey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I guess you bought that book after we broke up huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah I did, because I wore out my first copy when I was with you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, yeah? Well uh, when we were going out, I read tons of porno magazines! ‘Sup?
lines.stage_direction: chases her Realizes a table of women overheard him. To that table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ross! How could you do that to an old man?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me ladies. I’m sorry?
lines.stage_direction: looking at the table To Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My massage client, Arthur? His daughter called and said that some guy that worked for me gave him a really weird massage this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gave him an extremely professional massage!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He said you poked at him with wooden spoons.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, so it wasn’t uh, a traditional massage. But I did give him accu-pressure with a pair of chopsticks. And, and I gently exfoliated him with, with a mop.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he’s never coming back! Okay? You just cost me eight dollars a week!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y’know what? This is your fault! You’re the one that didn’t move his-his appointment.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it’s my fault?! You didn’t have to massage him! You could’ve sent him away! You could’ve not rolled Tonka trucks up and down his back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He said he liked that!! Oh you’re right, you’re right. I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, what are you massaging an old man for?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: His daughter was hot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Gotcha.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey and Ross are there as Rachel enters and sees Joey sitting there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen umm, I’ve been thinking, it’s not fair for me to ask you to spend all of your money on our wedding. I mean, you work, you work really hard for that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ehh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Eh, you work for that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I thought about it too, and I’m sorry. I think we should spend all of the money on the wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You do?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I’m putting my foot down. Yeah look, when I proposed I told you that I would do anything to make you happy, and if having the perfect wedding makes you happy then, then that’s what we’re gonna do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you’re so sweet. Oh, but wait, what about our, what about the future and stuff?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh, forget about the future and stuff! So we only have two kids, y’know? We’ll pick our favorite and that one will get to go to college.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You thought about that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How many kids were we gonna have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, four, a boy, twin girls and another boy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What else did you think about?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, stuff like where’d we live, y’know? Like a small place outside the city, where our kids could learn to ride their bikes and stuff. Y’know, we could have a cat that had a bell on it’s collar and we could hear it every time it ran through the little kitty door. Of course, we’d have an apartment over the garage where Joey could grow old.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know what? I-I don’t want a big, fancy wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure you do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I want everything you just said. I want a marriage.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I love you so much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you. Hey listen umm, when, when you were talkin’ about our future you said cat, but you meant dog right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, totally!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh good.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is looking at the wedding book as Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello, Zelda.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who are you supposed to be?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The vicar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you even know what a vicar is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like a goalie, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Look Joey, it’s enough all right?! You keep making these stupid jokes and this sleazy innuendoes and it’s—I’m not—it’s just not funny anymore!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I’m sorry. Rach I—Rach I’m sorry. Okay? I’m sorry! Maybe I can make up for it by, taking you roughly in the barn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right! Y’know what? That’s it! You wanna do it?! Let’s do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s right, I wanna do it with you! I’ve been trying to fight it, but you just said all the right things.
lines.stage_direction: starting to move closer to him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I-I-I-I did?
lines.stage_direction: nervously backing away He puts a stool in front of her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Ohh, I’ve been waitin’ so long to get on that body!
lines.stage_direction: moves the stool out of the way

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This body?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah that’s right! Come on Joey; sex me up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, you’re startin’ to sound like the butcher’s wife there in-in chapter seven.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on now, don’t keep me waiting. Get those clothes off! But, I would keep that helmet on because you’re in for a rough ride!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t want to, I’m scared.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is balancing her checkbook as Joey enters from his room wearing a hockey helmet, gloves, and shin guards.
Rachel walks away, pleased with her self.

title: The One With Phoebe’s Cookies

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you kidding? Okay, I’ll give you a hint; I’ll give you a hint.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Eyes! No, no. Your eyes! No. Chandler’s eyes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I got glasses!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you-you’ve always had glasses.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I didn’t!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, did-didn’t you use to have a pair? They were really round, burgundy, and they made you look kind of umm…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Feminine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweetie, I think the glasses look great. They make you look really sexy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You didn’t think I used to wear glasses, right?
lines.stage_direction: sitting down on the arm of her chair

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course!
lines.stage_direction: She mouths, "I have no idea," to the rest of the gang.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel, Phoebe, Joey, Ross, and Monica are all there as Chandler enters wearing glasses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what do you guys want for an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s okay Pheebs, we’re not having a party or anything, so you don’t have to get us…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: If someone wants to give us a present, we don’t want to deprive them of that joy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y’know what you should get ‘em? One of those little uh, portable CD players.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I already have one.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not unless someone borrowed it and left it at the gynecologist.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and-and-and by someone, she means Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, I know I what I want!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What we want honey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, you don’t want this. I want to have your grandmother’s cookie recipe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the chocolate chip cookie recipe?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the one that my grandmother made me swear on her deathbed that I would never let out of our family?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dying people say the craziest things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wanted it for years! I was gonna make cookies for my children.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Break my heart—Oh, all right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I’m gonna be the mom that makes the world’s best chocolate chip cookies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Our kids are gonna be fat aren’t they.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahoy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How’s the boat?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! I’m finally getting into this sailing stuff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, so you finally took it out of the marina huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why would I do that? It took three guys to get the thing in there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you don’t sail your boat, what do you do on it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it’s great! It’s a great place to just kinda, sit, hang around, drink a few beers, eat some chips.
lines.stage_direction: He says that as he sits, hangs around, drinks a beer, and eats a chip.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it’s good that you finally have a place to do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know Joey, I could teach you to sail if you want.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You could?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I’ve been sailing my whole life. When I was fifteen my dad bought me my own boat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your own boat?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What?! He was trying to cheer me up! My pony was sick.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Rachel, Phoebe, Chandler, and Monica are making some sandwiches.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you know what I was thinkin’?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing, I just like to go like this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Chandler, what are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh why, do you have a lecture?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then free as a bird. What’s up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My dad wanted to know if you wanted to play racquetball with us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! That’s great! Dad must really like you, he doesn’t ask just anyone to play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah and he didn’t really ask for you, he asked for Chancy, I assumed he meant you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, did-did you correct him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I thought it would be more fun this way.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so cool, maybe this is something you can do every week.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Or you can sit with him on the front porch and make sure no one steals the trash cans. He does that every week too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, just so you know, you-you have to let him win.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He hates to lose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no problem, maybe I’ll play with my left hand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re not a lefty?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does anybody know me?!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler and Monica are there. He takes off his glasses and starts chewing on the ear piece.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s wrong Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just went to my old apartment to get you the-the cookie recipe and the stupid fire burned it up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!! Why didn’t you make a copy and-and keep it in a fireproof box and keep it at least a hundred yards from the original?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I’m normal! That was the one legacy my grandmother left me, and I know you wanted it as an engagement present.
lines.stage_direction: pauses as he figures out how to answer that

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, we have to get you an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don’t worry about it Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No one got me an engagement present.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, here I wish you health and happiness.
lines.stage_direction: She hands Monica a cookie in a plastic baggie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: An old cookie?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is what happens when you don’t register for gifts!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See no-no, I made a batch and I froze it, and this is the only one left.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can’t accept this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ‘Cause it’s gross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Wait! I think I can figure out the recipe from this cookie! I do stuff like this at work all the time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! I bet I can do it.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters, walks up to Monica, and exhales exasperatedly.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, we owe you a present.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Two! I’ve been engaged twice!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler looks over and sees Ross glaring at them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look at this clown! Just because he’s got a bigger boat he thinks he can take up the whole river. Get out of the way jackass! Who names his boat Coast Guard anyway?
lines.stage_direction: drinking a beer Yelling To Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is the Coast Guard.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are they doing out here? The coast’s all the way over there.

scenes.scene_description: The Mr. Bowmont (Joey’s boat), she’s shown to be in one of the waterways around New York, but in reality she’s in a sound stage on the Warner Bros. lot and we see New York from the water in some rather poor green screen shots. Rachel and Joey are on board.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, just ignore the boats all right? We’re not finished with the lesson yet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I’m just gonna go over the basic points just one more time, are you ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Rach, not again. I got it! Okay? Let’s start sailing, and I want to go over there where that boatload of girls is! Yo-ho-ho!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay. Is that what you want to do? You wanna go over and give a little shout out to the old, hot chickas? Okay, let’s do that Sailor Joe. Quick question though, what’s this called?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, boat rope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wrong! How do you get the mainsail up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, rub it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. What do you do if I say we are coming about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’d say, come again. No-no, wait I-I-I know this one, I know this one, uh…

scenes.scene_description: Points to the coast, meanwhile there is coast behind him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Time’s up, now your dead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And deaf!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you just go on and make your little jokey-jokes, but if you do not know what you are doing out at sea you will die at sea. Am I getting through to you sailor?!
lines.stage_direction: She punctuates each word by slapping him on the forehead.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don’t just say yes! This isn’t a game, Joey you can really get hurt out here. Okay, so do you want to pay attention or do you want to die?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I want to make a ship to shore call to Chandler.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel blasts an air horn in his ear.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I definitely taste nutmeg.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don’t? Well, that’s the difference between a professional and a layman.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That and arrogance.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How was sailing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t want to talk about it. Y’know, you could’ve at least saved me a whole cookie.
lines.stage_direction: He grabs what’s left of the cookie and pops in his mouth.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Women are mean!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe that! Now the only thing left of my grandmother’s legacy is this crumb. I wish you a long and happy marriage.
lines.stage_direction: She picks it up with her finger and holds it out to Monica.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! How was it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I had a great time! Umm, Chancy on the other hand…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I will tell the story! It was going great. I let him win. We were bonding. He even said I could call him dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And what did he ask you not to call him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Daddy. All right look, here’s the story. Well, we had just finished playing racquetball and we were gonna take a steam. I walk into the steam room and it was really steamy. So I take off my glasses and that’s when in happened.
lines.stage_direction: Flashback to Chandler about to enter the steam room as he does the voice-over. The flashback shows his glasses fog up and him trying to find his way around the steam filled room. He takes off his glasses.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Over here.
lines.stage_direction: You can see Ross sitting at the far wall.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Have a seat son. Hey!!
lines.stage_direction: You can see Mr. Geller sitting closer to the door as Chandler walks over and sits in his lap. Chandler quickly jumps off and sits next to him with a shocked expression on his face.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Phoebe are trying to determine the cookie recipe by eating small pieces.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God Chandler! I can’t believe it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You gave my father a lap dance!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why do they put so much steam in there?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ‘Cause otherwise they’d have to call it the room room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why? Okay? Why? Wh-wh-why did that have to happen?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, it’s not that big a deal!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not that big a deal? There…there was touching of things.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, I know you wanted to bond with my dad, but did you really have to bond to that part?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen, I’m sure that dad doesn’t care. He probably thought this was funny; he’ll be telling this story for years!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t want him to tell this story for years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, but he will. He still tells the story how Monica tried to escape from fat camp.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wasn’t escaping.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then how did you get caught in the barbed wire?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was trying to help out a squirrel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You were trying to eat it!

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler is finished telling everyone what happened.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If that is your father calling to tell this story then the marriage is off!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on. Hello? I’m sorry you have the wrong number. Okay, I’ll call you later dad. I love you.
lines.stage_direction: takes the phone from Ross Answering phone Listens Listens Whispering Hangs up.

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, I’m off to see your dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, aren’t you a little over dressed?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and-and you better make sure he tips you this time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I figured I would try to convince him not to tell the story anymore, and I figure the best way to do that is face to face—And by face I don’t mean his lap. And by face, I don’t mean my ass.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey are you getting Monica and Chandler an engagement present?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know. Y’know, they didn’t get us anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well hello! So, when are we gettin’ back out on the water matey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, I don’t know the boat way to say this, but uh never!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because! You’re mean on the boat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? I was just trying to teach you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, lesson learned! Rachel is mean!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeeeeeep… Yep-yep-yep-yep-yep. I remember when she took out on her dad’s boat she wouldn’t let me help at all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me, I wanted you to help, but you couldn’t move your arms because you were wearing three life jackets.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You have to respect the sea!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look Joey, I’m sorry if-if you thought that was mean, but I gotta tell ya something. That was not mean. Okay, my father is mean. He used to yell at me all the time on the boat, I mean it was horrible. I was just being a good teacher.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Does a good teacher say, "Put down the beer pinhead!?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, does a good student drink seven beers during his first lesson?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Six and a half! You knocked that last one out of my hand! Remember?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I didn’t want you to get hit by the boom!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it hit me anyway! And it would’ve hurt a lot less if I had finished that last beer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, y’know what? I-I’m sorry. I will try to tone it down and uh stop yelling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You won’t boss me around anymore?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I won’t boss you around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you’ll be nice?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And, I’ll be nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you’ll be topless?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And—Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you want me to learn?!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler, Ross, and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, here’s batch 22. Ohh, maybe these’ll taste a little like your grandmother’s. This has a little bit of orange peel, but no nutmeg.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let’s give it a shot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Man, I have not made this many cookies since I was in the ninth grade.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, what was that for? Like a bake sale?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, just a Friday night.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is trying out different cookie recipes. Ross and Phoebe are the tasting group.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, these are pretty good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but not as good as batch 17.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Which one was that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The ones we had right after you almost threw up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah! Batch 17 was good. I did not like batch 16. I’m okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are there anymore from the good batch? ‘Cause we could just work off of those.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, ooh yeah, I think there is one from batch 17 left, uh… It’s batch 16! 16 people! Get out of the way!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a cookie and takes a big bite out of it and doesn’t like it. Gets up and runs for the bathroom.

scenes.scene_description: They all take a bite.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Joey honey, you’re doing really good! All right, now I’m just gonna need you to step to the port side. Remember? Remember how we talked about the port side?
lines.stage_direction: Joey pauses as he tries to remember which side is the port side.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s left sweetie, but that’s okay sweetie, that’s a tough one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know why you just don’t say left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, go to the left. The left!
lines.stage_direction: Joey goes starboard or sits on the right side of the boat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just sit over there!!
lines.stage_direction: yelling Points to the port side.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! Okay, you’re yelling again! See that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No-no, no-no-no, very quiet, said with love, no yelling.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y’know what? Since I’m here, I think I’m gonna have me a little beer on the port side.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay Joey, we’re luffing a little bit, so could you tighten up the cunningham?
lines.stage_direction: The mainsail has started to flap in the wind and has stopped working efficiently; she wants him to tighten it so that it starts working again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, wow, you just said a bunch of stuff I didn’t know there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, come on! We just went over this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, y’know, when we did that was when that bird was flying overhead with the fish in his mouth. Did you see it? It was gross!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! All right?! I did not see the bird! I did not see the fish! I did not see the piece of Styrofoam that was shaped like Mike Tyson! I did not , because I was trying to teach you how to sail a boat! Which obviously is an impossible thing to do!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right that’s it! You’re yelling and I don’t see you taking your top off! I quit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What do you mean you quit?! You can’t quit!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because you’re not finished yet and I won’t have it! Greens do not quit!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Greens? I’m a Tribbiani! And Tribbianis quit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, wait did I—I just said Greens don’t quit didn’t I? Did I just say Greens don’t quit?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yes! You did and you’re still yelling at me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No! No! I’m not yelling at you, I’m just yelling near you. Oh God Joey, ohh I’m my father. Oh my God, this is horrible! I’ve been trying so hard not to be my mother I did not see this comin’. Oh, Joey, I’m sorry. I’m so sorry. I just wanted you to learn.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, hey I did learn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Come on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awww…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it’s okay. I know what a mainsail is. I know, I know to duck when the boom comes across. I-I know port is right.
lines.stage_direction: Points to it. It’s the larger sail.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Left.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damnit!

scenes.scene_description: The Mr. Bowmont, its Joey’s second lesson with Rachel as the resident sailing expert.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know, I bet it would actually make my grandmother very happy to know that we’re trying to figure out her recipe. I bet she’s l-l-lookin’ up at us and smiling right now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Looking up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah—No, she was really nice to me, but she’s in hell for sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I’ve tried everything. I give up. I guess I’m not gonna be the mom who makes the world’s best chocolate cookies. I do make the best duck confit with broccoli rabe. Kids love that right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aww, Pheeb, come on isn’t there any relative that would have the recipe? What about, what about your sister?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no-no, no, I made a promise to myself that the next time I would talk to Ursula would be over my dead body. And that’s not happening ‘til October 15 th , 2032.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s the day you’re gonna die? See—darnit, I’ve got shuffleboard that day.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s what you think.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I mean what about friends of your grandmother’s? Wouldn’t they have the recipe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, y’know I may have relatives in France who would know. My grandmother said she got the recipe from her grandmother, Nesele Tolouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What was her name?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nesele Toulouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nestle Tollhouse ?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you Americans always butcher the French language.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, is this the recipe?
lines.stage_direction: grabbing a bag of Nestle Tollhouse chocolate chips Tosses her the bag.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes!! Ohh.

lines.stage_direction: grabbing Phoebe They go into her office and she closes the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you hired yourself a little treat did ya?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right I know, I know how it looks Pheebs, but I’m telling you…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But-but you know you cannot get involved with your assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I know that. I know that. And I know that hiring him was probably not the smartest thing that I’ve ever done. But I’m telling you, from this moment on I swear this is strictly professional. Yes?
lines.stage_direction: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Hey Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Cute assistant! What’s his story? Is he…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gay? Yeah.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe rounds the corner.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Terry.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: Joey Tribbiani! I’m surprised your big head could fit through our small halls! I gotta go Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait! Terry! Wait—Look—Wait I-I… Look, I’m really sorry about before. I was an idiot thinking I’m too big to audition for you. You gotta give me another chance.

lines.speaker: Terry
lines.text: I can’t help you Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait! Terry! Please! Look, I just lost my other job. Okay? You have no idea how much I need this. Please, help me out, for old times sake.

scenes.scene_description: Terry’s office, Joey has come to beg for a second chance.

lines.speaker: Dr. Stryker Remoray
lines.text: Good morning. Drake, it’s your brother Stryker. Can you hear me?
lines.stage_direction: He walks over to the bed, leans down, and whispers to him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m back baby! Ha-ha-ha!
lines.stage_direction: jumping up and removing the bandages

scenes.scene_description: Stryker enters, only it’s not Joey playing him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know, in my defense, umm there was no glitter on the macaroni and very little glue.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And in my defense, the cleaning lady came on to me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You have no trouble telling time now right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Quick! What time is it?!
lines.stage_direction: Holds his watch in front of her face.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know! Time to kiss a guy maybe?! What are you laughing at Pampers ?
lines.stage_direction: Ross laughs. He stops laughing and glares at her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know when I said that because we’re getting married that we should share everything and not have any secrets?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah that was stupid. Let’s not do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, absolutely.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And! We should keep all the stuff uh, we told each other secret from everybody else.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, definitely!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, if you’ll excuse me, I-I’m gonna go hang out with some people who don’t know the Space Mountain story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then, I’d steer clear of Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, and not that you would, but I wouldn’t hang out with…all the guys in my office.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, and Ross are sitting there and not talking to each other.
Ross storms out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!
lines.stage_direction: noticing him Puts the pictures away.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Do you have a minute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, sure, what’s up?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I got asked out twice today when I was at lunch…by guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh really?!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah. Did you tell someone that I was gay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, did you not want people to know that?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: But I’m not gay. And I especially wouldn’t want you to think I was gay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why’s that?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I don’t think I should say.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, you can say. Come on, I don’t want you to feel like you can’t tell me things.
lines.stage_direction: Motions for him to sit down.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ‘Kay.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Well…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I’d love to ask out your friend Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, she’s gay.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s office, she’s looking at a picture of Tag when he knocks and enters.

title: The One With The Engagement Picture

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What’s the matter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Someone on the subway licked my neck! Licked my neck!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Willie’s still alive!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you guys doing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, my mom called, they’re gonna run our engagement announcement in the local paper, so we’re looking for a good picture of us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oooh, I’m afraid that does not exist.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s not true, there are great pictures of us!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, there are great pictures of you standing next to a guy who’s going like this…
lines.stage_direction: Makes what can only be described as a toothy frown. Henceforth, this shall be known as The Face.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! That’s the creep that you’re with at the Statue of Liberty .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know what it is, I just can’t take a good picture.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, here’s a great one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I’m not in that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, but look at me all tan.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, why don’t you guys go, get portraits done by a professional photographer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s a good idea! I bet they have one of those wind machines! Y’know…
lines.stage_direction: Does the whole hair blowing in the wind model type poses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that’s great! Next to that, Chandler won’t look so stupid.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler what do you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, but I should warn you, I’m not going. I’m going.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica and Phoebe are going through a bunch of pictures as Chandler enters.
Does The Face while saying that last part.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude, that reverse lay-up! Oh…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How about those three pointers?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Amazing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And those guys were this close to lettin’ us play this time too.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel is siting on the couch as Ross and Chandler enter after playing basketball.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey look-look, Phoebe’s talking to uh, Cute Coffeehouse Guy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you guys call him Cute Coffeehouse Guy, we call him Hums While He Pees.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and we call Ross Lingers In The Bathroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys, Hums While He Pees just asked me out!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, I thought that guy was married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He is! But he’s getting divorced—Ross! Maybe you know him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s not a club.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, if this guy’s going through a divorce, is it such a good idea to start going out with him?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, divorced men are not bad men!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They have that on the napkins at the club.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I gotta get back to work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don’t have to be back for a half-hour!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but, my assistant Tag does sit-ups in the office during lunch. Ohh! I could just spread him on a cracker.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rach, if you have a crush on this guy, why would you hire him? I mean y’know you can’t date him right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, I know that. I know that. Although, we made a joke that we spend so much time together he should call me his work wife.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Soon he’ll be able to call you, that lady he knew who got fired.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am not gonna get fired, because I’m not gonna act on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you wouldn’t mind if he was dating someone else?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? Is he? He is! Isn’t he? He’s dating that slut in marketing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe I should open a divorced men’s club.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dude that is so sad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I could put uh-uh a basketball court in the back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could I play?

scenes.scene_description: They both get dejected and go sit down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no sit-ups today Tag?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I just did them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well drop and give me ten more!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, I-I had a drink with lunch. Did those cost reports come in?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah, I filled them out last night?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, great could you make me four copies of those?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Outer Office, she’s returning from lunch to see Tag not doing his sit-ups.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Hey Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh, hi! Hi! Melissa, what’s up? I’m just uh, about to umm, go out to the store to get some stuff to put in my backpack. Y’know, like dried fruit and granola and stuff. What’s up?
lines.stage_direction: startled She has put on the backpack.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Umm, is Tag here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Why?

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Oh, I was gonna talk to him about doing something tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! Got a little crush on Tag there do ya?

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Well, we’ve been flirting back and forth, but I was hoping that tonight it would turn into something a little more than that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, whoa-whoa easy there Melissa! This ain’t a locker room, okay? But, y’know I remember him saying that-that he had plans tonight.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. All right, back to work.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Hey! Isn’t that Tag’s backpack.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah Melissa, I don’t want to be known as the uh, office bitch, but I will call your supervisor.

scenes.scene_description: He gets up to make the copies leaving Rachel alone with his stuff. She notices his sweater in his backpack and holds it up to her nose as Melissa, a coworker, walks up.
Melissa beats a hasty retreat.

lines.speaker: The Photographer
lines.text: Great! That’s great Monica! Great! Now, Chandler, you want to give us a smile?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: The Photographer
lines.text: I’m sorry, is the seat uncomfortable?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I am.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, listen to me sweetie, I know you can do this. Okay? You have a beautiful smile.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! All right, maybe you don’t have to smile. Let’s try something else. Let’s try umm, try looking sexy.
lines.stage_direction: They turn to the camera, and Chandler does The Face again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: You’ll have to see it, I can’t describe the face he makes, but it isn’t good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or not.

scenes.scene_description: A Portrait Studio, Chandler and Monica are trying to take their engagement picture. Monica has a beautiful smile, while Chandler isn’t.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Joey! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, well I’ve got an audition down the street and I spilled sauce all over the front of my shirt. You got an extra one?
lines.stage_direction: Removes his hand to reveal a huge sauce stain.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure. Umm…here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great. You got anything that’s not Ralph Lauren ?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I don’t think so Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I guess this will be fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, listen umm, what-what are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing, why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How would you feel about taking out my assistant Tag? I’ll pay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh, Rach I got to say it’s gonna take a lot of money for me to go out on a date with a dude.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not asking you to go on a date with him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? ‘Cause I could kinda use the money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, just-just he-he’s new in town and I know he doesn’t have any guy friends. Just take him to like a ball game or something. I’ll really appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, no problem. Ooh—Hey, donuts!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: He grabs a jelly donut, takes a bite, and guess what he spills all over himself. He tries to clean it up and smears it all over the shirt.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Office, Joey is knocking on the door holding a hand over a spot on his shirt.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. Let’s try a look…of far off…wonderment. Okay, we’ll-we’ll gaze into our future and we’ll think about our marriage and the days to come. Chandler! What is the matter with your face?! I mean this picture is supposed to say "Geller and Bing to be married," not "Local woman saves drowning moron!" Hey! Don’t laugh at him! He’s my drowning moron!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler is still not getting it. The photographer laughs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aww!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s it! Take it! Take it! Take it!

scenes.scene_description: A Portrait Studio, Chandler and Monica are still trying to get the picture taken.
Chandler turns to the camera and does The Face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like this one. It seems to say, "I love you and that’s why I have to kill you."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They can’t all be bad. Find the one where you make your bedroom eyes. Ohh, there it is.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God! Those are my bedroom eyes?! Why did you ever sleep with me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you really want to pull at that thread?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, and Ross are going over the picture proofs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m having a really good time!

lines.speaker: Hums While He Pees
lines.text: Me too! I’m sorry that guy in the subway licked your neck.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh. No that’s okay, he’s a friend.

lines.speaker: Hums While He Pees
lines.text: Hey uh, I don’t mean to be presumptuous but I have these two tickets to the ballroom dancing finals tomorrow night if you want to go?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I… Well y’know I-I mean I missed the-the semi-finals, so I’d just be lost.

lines.speaker: Hums While He Pees
lines.text: I know it’s really lame, but I got these tickets from my boss and—Oh no! No! No! My God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, don’t freak out. I’ll go.

lines.speaker: Hums While He Pees
lines.text: No it’s… Uh, my ex-wife Whitney is out there. I cannot deal with her right now. That woman is crazy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I know. Hold on. Hey Ross?
lines.stage_direction: She walks over to the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, umm that’s Whitney , Kyle’s ex-wife out there, now do you think that you can y’know divert her so that we can slip out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well okay but I have two tickets to the ballroom dance finals.
lines.stage_direction: She holds up the tickets that Kyle gave her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I don’t think so Pheebs. All right, I’ll do it. But just because you’re a friend.
lines.stage_direction: Pause Grabs the tickets and heads to divert Whitney.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters with Hums While He Pees also known as Kyle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Ginger.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right! I want my key back!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t have it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s right there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh, okay Sherlock!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I’m sorry but you-you-you better go Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, well I just wanted to say thank you though for diverting Kyle’s ex.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah—No—You’re welcome. We’ll talk about it later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment, Ross is dancing around his living room as Phoebe enters, catching and startling him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Whitney.

lines.speaker: Whitney
lines.text: Hi Ross! You ready for breakfast?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep. Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Kyle’s ex-wife? You were supposed to divert her not date her!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! I’m sorry, but can you give me a second while I talk to this woman, who by the way did not spend the night.
lines.stage_direction: opens the door and to Whitney

lines.speaker: Whitney
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. I did divert her and we ended up having a great time! Okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Watching ballroom dancing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! That’s where we realized we were both super cool people!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well look-look, okay Ross, Kyle just told me some really bad stuff about her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Like she’s really mean, and she’s over critical, and-and—No! She will paint a room a really bright color without even checking with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And! She uses sex as a weapon!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine! Thank you for warning me. At breakfast I’ll be on full alert for room painting and sex weapons.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re still gonna go out with her?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, didn’t you just hear what I said?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs come on! I mean, consider the source! Of course her ex-husband’s gonna say that stuff. Now, if you’ll excuse me…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No listen to me! She is crazy !

lines.speaker: Whitney
lines.text: Uh, your door isn’t sound proof.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You see? Nothing is good enough for her!

scenes.scene_description: Ross opens the door to reveal Whitney standing there.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Tag! Hey, so did you have fun with uh, with Joey last night?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh yeah! We went to the Knicks game.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh that’s nice.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Then we went to this bar and he hooked us up with all these women!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wo-women? You mean like old women?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Well kinda old, like 30.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: And I never used to be able to just talk to girls in bars, but I got like 20 phone numbers last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s great! Wow man, so Joey must’ve really taught you some stuff huh?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: A little.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Office, Tag is arriving as Rachel is standing there.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: How you doin’?
lines.stage_direction: To her, in the Joey voice

scenes.scene_description: A beautiful women walks up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: See? That’s a great smile! Easy. Natural. Now, pretend I have a camera. You’re changing it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can’t help it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right, all right, all right, you wanna know what I do when I take resume shots?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Borrow money from me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, first—first of all, you want to make it look spontaneous. I look down , look down, keep looking down; then I look up. See? All right, now you try. Look down , you’re looking down, keep looking down…
lines.stage_direction: Looks down Looks up and smiles. Chandler looks down

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why is there jelly on your shoe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I had a donut.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So uh, heard you had some fun with Tag last night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! That guy’s all right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and you had fun teaching him how to be all Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, all the women.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey well, you can’t teach someone to be good with women. Y’know, that’s why I never had any luck with Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m right here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, would-would you mind just not going out with him again? Okay, just the idea of you and he and all these women, it’s just—And I know he’s my assistant and I can’t date him—but it just bothers me, all right?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! No-no-no-no, you can’t take him away from me! I got a great partner to pick up girls with! Finally!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m still right here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, will you, will you at least tell him how hollow and unsatisfying this, dating tons of women thing is!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I just don’t want him to meet anybody until I am over my crush—And I will get over it. It’s-it’s not like I love him, it’s just physical! But—I mean I get crushes like this all the time! I mean hell, I had a crush on you when I first met ya!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, Monica told me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you have a crush on me, when you first met me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Sure.
lines.stage_direction: Joey nods his head questioningly and Rachel nods no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you people not see me?!
lines.stage_direction: He waves his arms around to get their attention.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, will you talk to him?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on! I’ll give you ten free Ralph Lauren shirts.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: One! No ten! You said ten! You can’t take that back!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is teaching Chandler how to smile. Chandler is smiling.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey Joey, you wanted to talk to me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know. You uh, you got something for me?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh, yeah, this is from Rachel.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey is there as Tag enters carrying a bag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ten. Okay. Now Tag there’s such a thing as to many women.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, for you!

scenes.scene_description: He hands Joey the bag and he quickly counts its contents.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! There you are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There I am!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, Joey said I uh, I needed to relax so he gave me an antihistamine.

lines.speaker: Monica
