lines.text: And she’s back in the game.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Ross pause while they picture the event.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Julie hi! Chandler Bing, I, I guess you remember me.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: Hello Skidmark.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s a nickname, I’ll explain later.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s pretty clear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, uh, I owe you a long overdue apology. I never should have broken up with you because you were overweight.

lines.speaker: Julie
lines.text: That’s why you broke up with me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You-you-you didn’t know that. Well, I guess my work here is done!

scenes.scene_description: A random apartment building, Chandler and Monica are knocking on the door of the woman from the beginning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, well first of all I would like to say that you both performed very well. Okay? You should be proud of yourselves. And-and I would also like to say that in this competition there are no losers. Well, except for Rachel—Damnit!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! I won!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry Rach, it was, it was really close.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then I demand a recount!
lines.stage_direction: Hmm, I wonder where I’ve heard that before.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Actually, it wasn’t that close.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Y’know what? No! No! You thing was so stupid anyway, this was ridiculous—We’re gonna flip a coin! All right?! Heads!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe gasps. She flips the coin. Looks at the coin and grunts in disgust.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The coins have finally forgiven me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y’know what? I hope Monica forgives you after you throw her, her vegetarian, voodoo, goddess circley shower!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rach, it’s gonna be okay! You guys are the best!
lines.stage_direction: running after her To Ross and Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Boy I tell ya, that judging stuff took a lot out of me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I was thinking about maybe going upstairs and taking a little nap on my couch.
lines.stage_direction: Raises his eyebrows, questioning Ross to see if he wants to join him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why-why would I care about that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No reason, I’m just saying that uh… That’s where I’ll be.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey and Ross are giving Phoebe and Rachel the results of their election. Somewhat faster than Florida I might add.
Joey gets up and heads for the door. After a pause Ross decides to join him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: As bad as that went I actually enjoyed myself. I think that I’m going to apologize for all of the stupid things I do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why don’t you just stop doing stupid things? Then you wouldn’t have to apologize.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I would really love it if could do both.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I…I have to ask.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you gonna break up with me if I get fat again?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you broke up with Julie Grath! How much weight could she have gained?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A hundred and forty-five pounds.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In one year?! My God what did she eat? Her-her family! That’s not the point.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look I know it was a stupid reason to break up with somebody, but I was 15!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well… That’s not the only time this was an issue. You remember when umm, you spent Thanksgiving with us? You called me fat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Okay, now wait a minute that was totally different.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You were not supposed to hear that! I said that behind you back!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What if I have babies, okay? I mean I’m gonna look different. I’m okay with that, but I’m not sure that you are!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look you have to realize I don’t think of you as a thin, beautiful woman. See this is one of things that I can apologize for later! Look, what I mean is you’re Monica! Okay? And I am in love with Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Keep going.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you can balloon up or you can shrink down and I will still love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Even if I shrink down to two inches tall?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’d carry you around in my pocket.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Skidmark’s still got a way with the ladies.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler and Monica have returned from Julie’s.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! I just want to apologize. I’m really sorry I was a baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s ridiculous Rachel, we were all babies once. Oh, you mean today.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, and y’know you-you deserve to win. And-and y’know I was thinking about it, if-if you’re Monica’s maid of honor that means I get to be yours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Oh, umm when-when Monica and Chandler got engaged I started putting some stuff together, y’know just in case…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that’s so sweet thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here is a book of poetry that I know Monica loves. And-and ohh God this is funny, look, this is a picture of one Halloween where she dressed up as a bride. And look, she made me carry her train, which was weird because I was Wonder Woman . Oh and here’s a little purse that I found. Y’know I just thought that maybe they could hold the rings in there.
lines.stage_direction: Shows Phoebe the picture. Hands her the purse

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And umm, vintage handkerchiefs y’know ‘cause, people cry at weddings. I’m just gonna grab a couple of these.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This stuff is great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I forgot this was in here. Umm, this was the uh garter that I was saving for my wedding and I wanted it to be Monica’s something borrowed and it’s blue. Yeah…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know Rach, I think that, I think you should be Monica’s made of honor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You do? Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I think it means more to you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But Pheebs, y’know you earned it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Its fine. I mean, this is something that you’ve been thinking about since you were what, 14?
lines.stage_direction: She’s referring to the Halloween picture.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I was ten. I just developed early.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Man alive!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, what’s going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, we just decided that Rachel is gonna be your maid of honor.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, wow! That’s great! Oh wow! We really have to start planning! I have, I have a lot of really specific ideas! We should probably get together like four times per week. You can come over to my place; we’ll get together before work! What do you say, 6:30, my place? I’m so excited!
lines.stage_direction: gasps Rachel and Monica hug. Runs out leaving Rachel completely stunned and Phoebe laughing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah okay, you laugh now, but she’s gonna be yours.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is there as Rachel enters carrying a shopping bag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great nap.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It really was.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Ross are napping together again and both wake up at the same time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude! What the hell are you doing?! God!

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly Rachel clears her throat and the camera cuts to the rest of the gang staring at them. Needless to say Joey and Ross are shocked and slowly turn their heads to see the gang.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me.
lines.stage_direction: slowly stands up and says quietly Exits.

scenes.scene_description: Heads for his room leaving Ross.

title: The One With Ross’s Book

lines.speaker: Directed by
lines.text: David Schwimmer

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Morning!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Oh, how was your date last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pretty good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh good. Ahhh! My God, sorry!
lines.stage_direction: She walks to the closed bathroom door, opens it, and finds a naked woman wrapped in a towel. She closes the door and confronts Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, really good. Anyway I gotta go; I’m late for work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-what?! You’re gonna leave this person with me?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah—Hey, don’t worry, she’s a terrific girl. And hey listen, could you do me a favor? When she comes out could you just mention that I’m not looking for a serious relationship; that’d be great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?! What?! Are you kidding?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just casually slip it in, y’know lay the groundwork. Tell her uh, I’m a loner—No! An outlaw! Tell her she doesn’t want to get mixed up with the likes of me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know what? That’s a lot to remember, can’t I just tell her you’re a pig?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I’m gonna call her later! Honest! Oh come on, Chandler used to do it! He’d even make the girl pancakes! Plus, he’d make extras and leave ‘em for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well forget it, I’m not telling that girl anything. That is not my responsibility.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine! Now, where’d we land on those pancakes?
lines.stage_direction: Pause She chases him out the door as his date emerges from the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, Joey kinda disabled it when I moved in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Hi. I don’t mean this to sound like high school, but did he say anything about me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you like some pancakes?

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is eating breakfast as Rachel enters having just woken up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: …Come on! Why are we here?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay take a guess.

scenes.scene_description: N.Y.U’s University Library, Ross is entering with Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The hot chicks?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, okay, I was typing names into the library computer earlier, y’know-y’know for fun, and I typed mine in and guess what came up? My doctoral dissertation! It’s here! Yeah, it’s right-it’s right down here! In the biggest library in the university!
lines.stage_direction: They start heading that way, towards a secluded section behind the racks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow that’s actually pretty cool.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh umm, there’s also a book here by a woman named Wendy Bagina. What is that?
lines.stage_direction: stopping quickly They both laugh, but stop when the hear moaning coming from the next aisle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sounds like two people are really enjoying the Dewey decimal system.

scenes.scene_description: An old woman pushes a cart full of books past.

lines.speaker: Female Student
lines.text: I’m so sorry!
lines.stage_direction: as they are moving past Ross

lines.speaker: Male Student
lines.text: Sorry!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You didn’t bring me here to do that, did you?

scenes.scene_description: They go around the last row of bookshelves and find a couple doing what college coeds do in secluded corners of university libraries. For those of you who don’t know what I’m talking about, let’s just say that clothing is undone. Ross gasps and the couple gets up and runs away.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She sent the chicken back again?!

lines.speaker: The Waitress
lines.text: She says it’s to dry now and she wants to come back here and explain to you exactly how she wants it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well fine! I want to meet this chicken expert! Send the Colonel in!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Lucky bastard! Janice.
lines.stage_direction: to the lobster Throws the lobster in and turns around to face the planet’s most annoying woman, next to Dr. Laura and Kathy Lee Gifford of course. She’s the most annoying female TV character however.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: How are you Ms. Hot Shot chef with the big fancy restaurant with the best chicken ever!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m fine.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ohh! What is that on your finger?! I’m blind!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh… Uh…

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: So, who’s the lucky guy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: OH…MY…GAWD! I am so sorry sweetie, are you okay? You didn’t tell her we were getting married, did you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, she saw the ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did she freak out?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, she was shocked when I told her, but then again so were most people.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, she actually has a boyfriend y’know herself, named Clark. Uh, she also kinda invited herself to our wedding. Clark too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You said no right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You said no right?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, she corned me! She asked if the wedding was in town! I mean, what was I supposed to do?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Lie!!! How hard is that?! The check’s in the mail! Oh your baby is so cute! I can’t wait to read your book Ross!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on! So she comes to the wedding! I mean it won’t be so bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do you think she’s just gonna sit there quietly? You don’t think she’s gonna want to make a toast? You don’t think she’s gonna want to grab the microphone and sing Part-time Lover ?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, she’s not gonna like the chicken that night either is she?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what? It’s gonna be okay. Y’know what? She’s probably not gonna even want to come.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! That was a lie ! See how easy that was?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So-so you would’ve just lied?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would it really have been that easy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good, so do it Saturday night because we’re going to dinner with her and Clark.

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s Restaurant Kitchen, Monica is cooking as a waitress enters carrying a plate of food that has been sent back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! What’s up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, who’s your friend? Hey!!
lines.stage_direction: Erin turns around to face Joey, startling him

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Hey Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Erin! Still here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, we ended up spending the day together and had such a great time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why wouldn’t ya? Erin is great! Then-then there’s you guys.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Ohh, listen. I’ve got to get going. Today was great, thanks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Bye Joey. Last night was fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I’ll uh, I’ll call ya.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh and I’ll call ya too!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Or I’ll call you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And call me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is coming back from work to find Phoebe, Rachel, and someone else with her back turned is there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, system kinda broke down huh?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey, I’m sorry I just couldn’t tell her all those things you wanted me to tell her. And y’know we got to talking and I…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We want you to marry her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She is so amazing! You have no idea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No idea? Who do you think brought her here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cupid.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, she’s so cool. She speaks four languages.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, do you know what guys want!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look Joey, come on she’s so perfect for you! I mean she’s sweet, she-she likes baseball, and she-she had two beers at lunch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My beers?! Look you guys, she’s a very nice girl. Okay? We had a good time, but I just—I don’t see it going anywhere.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but you always say that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, maybe if you gave this girl a chance it would go somewhere.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look I’m sorry you guys, I-I just don’t think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whatever.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, don’t start judging me! Huh? You’re the one who’s in love with her assistant! Huh? And you, you’re the one having the affair with the guy who keeps the pigeons on the roof!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Secret affair!

scenes.scene_description: Joey closes the door behind her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: People are doing it in front of my book!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My doctoral dissertation is in the library at school, I went to see it, and there were students makin’ babies right in the middle of the Paleontology section!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Did you get to see anything good?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me ask you something, at your school was there a like uh a place on campus where students went to uh, fool around.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, there was. It was—there the corner of the library where-where all these dusty books that nobody ever read—Yes, there was.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Because people kept showing up, I think it’s like uh-a thing!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now hold on a second, fifth floor against that back wall?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh for cryin’ out loud!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, so we should go catch our movie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well now what’s the rush?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I like to see the previews. The candy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well look who’s here!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Erin.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Hey Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Well, we were just about to take off and see a movie. Oh no!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: What’s wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Phoebe, we forgot that party we have to go to.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What party?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who’s birthday party?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, and how is Allison?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute! Why don’t you guys do something?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, look how that worked out.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Rachel and Joey are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me. Hi, I’m a professor here. Do you know the Paleontology section, fifth floor, stack 437?

lines.speaker: The Librarian
lines.text: Well, yes! Just give me five minutes, I just have to find someone to cover my shift.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No!! No! Can I speak to someone in charge please?!
lines.stage_direction: The librarian brings his boss over.

lines.speaker: The Head Librarian
lines.text: How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, I was wondering if it is possible to increase security in the Paleontology section? See I-I wrote a book up there and instead of reading it people are-are-are well, rolling around in front of it.

lines.speaker: The Head Librarian
lines.text: We are aware of the problem you are referring too. But as far as increasing security, I’m afraid the library is very understaffed. I, I can’t help you.
lines.stage_direction: He turns to look at the previous librarian.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, fine. Fine! If-if I’m the only person with any appreciation of the sanctity of the written word, I’ll go up there and defend it myself! And don’t you follow me!
lines.stage_direction: Starts to do so, but stops and to the previous librarian

scenes.scene_description: The Library, Ross enters and heads to the desk to talk to the male librarian on duty.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How did it go with Erin?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, unbelievable! We had the best time!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!! Oh so, you’re not, you’re not mad at us anymore?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No! No! You guys were totally right! This is so much better than the first time we went out. Y’know? That was so awkward, we were really nervous.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Didn’t you sleep together?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah that really calms me down. And! We have so much in common! She loves sandwiches, sports, although she is a Met fan, not much of an issue now but if were ever to have kids, well that’s a…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Listen to you talkin’ about having kids. Oh my Joey. Oh, please don’t get married before I do.
lines.stage_direction: gasps She goes over and hugs him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Phoebe are doing dishes as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh. Oh I just cannot believe Clark stood me up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He might still show up.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, what are you, stupid? It’s been three hours.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that all?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I should just go on to happier things, okay? Umm, why don’t you tell about your lovely wedding?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well actually uh, there was something we wanted to tell you about the wedding. Um, it’s going to be a small ceremony. Uh, tiny! We’re not even sure why we’re having it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s actually going to be just family.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh…wait…you two think of me as family?! Oh, I have to ask you something now and be honest; do you want me to sing Careless Whisper or Lady In Red ?

scenes.scene_description: A Restaurant, Monica and Chandler are on their date with Janice and Clark, however Clark is a no show and Janice is crying the Mississippi River about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How can you say that?! The Mets have no closer!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: What about Benitez?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What about Game 1 of the Series?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: What about shut up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You shut up! I love arguing with her. I’ll be right back.
lines.stage_direction: To Phoebe and Rachel To Erin

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: Joey goes to get some coffee and Erin sits down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So how’s it goin’ with Joey?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Uh, okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay? Wait okay, tell-tell me that you like him, please? I mean tell me that you like him.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Look, he’s a really great guy and I know that you really want this to work out, but I just don’t see this having a future.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But you said that you liked him! I mean what happened?! Did ya just change your mind?!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Kinda.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then change it back!

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: I’m sorry I… It’s just there’s no real spark.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No spark? Didn’t you sleep together?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh, tramp!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Does Joey have any idea?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: I really don’t think he does. And y’know what? Maybe you guys could help clue him in. Y’know, tell him I’m-I’m not interested in a serious relationship or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you mean like that you’re kind of a loner.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Yeah! That would be great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, and maybe that you’re a real
lines.stage_direction: She says something in Italian, and it doesn’t matter what she said. It’s not important so I don’t need everyone who speaks Italian telling me what she said.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: I’m sorry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh well, I guess Italian isn’t one of the four languages you speak.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! You wanna go?

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Yeah, let’s go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. See you guys later.

lines.speaker: Erin
lines.text: Bye guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, see ya.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, thank you so much.
lines.stage_direction: whispers to Phoebe and Rachel They both exit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow. Well, I guess it was Cupid who brought her here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, just a regular old flying dwarf.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey is entering and arguing baseball with Erin. Phoebe and Rachel are already there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes? Yes?! How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Yeah, we were…we were just looking around.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, you’re-you’re fellow scholars. What exactly were you looking for, hmm? Perhaps, perhaps Dr. Chester Stock’s musings on the Smiledon Californicus?
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a book from the shelf behind him

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Uhh….

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah… Ah…Get out of here! Uh, meeting someone? Or-or are you just here to brush up on Marion’s views on evolution?
lines.stage_direction: The couple retreats. Ross starts looking through the previously mentioned book as a beautiful woman walks into the section.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Uh, actually I find Marion’s views far to progressionist.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I find Marion’s views far to progressionist.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I’m sorry, who are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m a professor here uh, Ross…Geller.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Ross Geller, why do I know that name? It’s uh—Wait! Did you write this?
lines.stage_direction: Grabs his book off of the shelf.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! You’re the person who checked out my book?!

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Y’know, you look nothing like I would’ve thought. You’re…you’re so young.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I uh, I skipped forth grade.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am very…very sorry.

scenes.scene_description: The library’s Paleontology section, Ross is patrolling as a couple walks up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So how was, how was your date?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it was great! I mean we walked all around the village. We went to this ice cream place, split a milkshake, 70/30 but still… And guess what, I’m thinking about taking her upstate to one of those bed and breakfasts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really? She said she wants to go away with you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no-no-no! It’s a surprise, but it’s gonna be tricky thought because she said she was gonna be pretty busy at work for a while.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Jo-Joey, look honey we-we need to talk okay? Umm, I kinda got the feeling from her today that uh, she’s not lookin’ for a serious relationship.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Where are you gettin’ this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, she told me. She said she’s kinda a loner.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No hey Rach, it’s cool okay? Y’know I’m a loner too! Right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Joey, y’know what? You are way to good for her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and honey I promise next time that I will just say good-bye and tell ‘em you’re not looking for a relationship.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No. Don’t do that, just next time make sure she really likes me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that too. Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you want some pancakes?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Finally!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Phoebe and Rachel are watching TV as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I say we go with Careless Whisper .

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Chandler and Monica are sitting at the kitchen table and are still trying to decide what to do about Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did she see us yet? Did she see us?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Janice, what umm, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Well umm, I thought I was going to go back to my apartment but then I just felt I couldn’t really be alone tonight. I was wondering if I could maybe stay here with you, just I really feel that I need to be with family.
lines.stage_direction: Joey walks into view of the open door behind Janice, sees her, gets a terrified look on his face, and flees in horror.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Our kids are gonna call her Aunt Janice aren’t they?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Please, it’s because otherwise I really don’t know what I might do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aren’t you just a tinsy bit curious?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Do you have any tissues?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, in-in-in the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We’ll just…we’ll just let her stay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, if we let her stay, she will stay forever !

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Kinda like your Barca lounger.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that what you’re thinking about right now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I never stop thinking about it.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Hey you guys, umm do either one of you want to get in there before I take my bath.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice, I’m sorry but umm, you can’t stay here tonight.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honestly? Our apartment is a hotbed for electromagnetic activity. Now Monica and I have been immunized, but sadly you have not.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door and Janice enters.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay, I’m going to need a comforter, but did you have a hypoallergenic one because otherwise I get very nasal. Do you have a cat? ‘Cause it’s already happening. Do you hear that?
lines.stage_direction: Makes some weird sound Makes a nasal sound She keeps making the nasal sound.

scenes.scene_description: And she does the laugh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God! You have to go!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because Chandler still has feelings for you!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: He does?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Say again?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s right. That’s right. And that is why you can’t stay here tonight. And probably why you shouldn’t come to the wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Feelings, such strong feelings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, I realize that his feelings may never completely go away, but you can.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh…my Gawd, I-I understand. I-I am so sorry, I’ll go. Good-bye Monica , I wish you a lifetime of happiness with him. Chandler, you call me when this goes in the pooper.
lines.stage_direction: Starts for the door. hugs her hugs him Hurries out.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler does the same nasal sound to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Don’t sweat it, I’ve got this section covered. Yeah, in fact I’ve got this little baby to shine in people’s eye——Okay, see you later.
lines.stage_direction: to the guard Turns on a mini-flashlight The guard walks away

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I just wanted to show Monica your book.

title: The One Where Chandler Doesn’t Like Dogs

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey everybody! Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no. No-no-no.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, are we keeping Thanksgiving a secret this year?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we're playing this game I learned at work. You have to name all the states in six minutes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? That's like insanely easy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, that's a lot harder than it sounds. You always forget at least one, or in some cases... fourteen .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's a stupid game and I wasn't playing against other people, so technically I didn't lose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? You forgot fourteen states?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nobody cares about the Dakotas.
lines.stage_direction: That’s true in so many ways, trust me, I’ve lived in one and been to the other.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay, time's up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I got 48.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's not bad, Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I got tired of naming states. So I decided to list the types of

lines.speaker: celery, and I have one
lines.text: regular celery.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so Rachel's got 48 and Phoebe has the lead in…vegetables, Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Say hello to the new champ of Chandler's dumb states game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, how many have you got?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fifty-six!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! How is New England not a state? Huh? They have a sports-team!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does South Oregon have a sports-team? There you go.

lines.text: How come we have one extra place setting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause you invited your assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, right. Sorry. But Tag's not coming; his girlfriend came into town, so he's spending Thanksgiving with her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Why didn't you tell me? I made him his own individual sweet potato stuffed pumpkin.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I was going to, but then I figured, you know... you're food is so delicious and perfect, you can never have too many of those pumpkin things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now you think I wouldn't enjoy that, because it is so fake, but I still do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Regular Celery! Oh, I already have that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Done! With time a-to-spare.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oooh that may be a New World's record

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I hate to lecture you guys, but it's kinda disgraceful, that a group of well-educated adults and Joey can't name all the states. Did you ever see a map, or one of those round, colorful things called "a globe?" Hmm?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Magellan? You got 46 states.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? That's impossible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 46. Wow! Who's well educated now, Mr. I-forgot-ten-states?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'm out of oven space. I'm gonna turn on Joey's. Please, watch him! Do not let Joey eat any of the food!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am only one man! Okay Ross, time is up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, just give me another minute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look Ross, if you don't know them by now, you will never know them, okay? That is the beauty of this game. It makes you want to kill yourself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This-this is crazy! I can do this! All right, uhh, I bet I can get all 50 before dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but if you can't…no dinner!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're on!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. Don't look at my list, Ross, 'cause there's a lot on there that you don't have.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, did you guys know, that your oven doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But the drawer full of take-out menus is okay, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, I'm gonna use yours, okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pshhshhh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler? Can you give me a hand?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, and Joey; do not let Ross look at any of the maps or the globe in your apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry, Chandler, it's not a globe of the United States.

lines.text: Hey you guys I'm gonna go out and take a walk.

lines.text: Oh, it's not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously, it's moving!

lines.text: What the hell is in there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's just my knitting that's all! Yes! I knit this. I'm very good.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's. Ross is sitting in the couch doing Chandler's game. Chandler is sitting in his barca-lounger. The girls are in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross’s apartment is nice! How come we don't hang out here more often?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. Maybe it's because it smells a little weird. It's like old pumpkins or something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's my pie!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which smells delicious!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-oh! Uh-oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We left Joey alone with the food! Yep! Yep, I knew it! There he is... feeding stuffing to a dog!
lines.stage_direction: Walks towards the window and looks out

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi Geller-Bing residence. How can I help?

lines.text: Phoebe, why is there a dog in our apartment?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, who's this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, there's a dog sitting on my couch!

lines.text: Tell her, I'm allergic, and I will sue!

lines.text: No, there's no dog here?

lines.text: Oh my god! Where are you?

lines.text: I'll be right there!

lines.text: Well, sure! Come in! Well, what-what happened to your girlfriend?

lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah, so she went back to Ohio.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohio!! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh! Where is the dog?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What dog? There-there's no dog here.

lines.text: Yeah that dog left!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe! Phoebe, open up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There's no dog in here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, we can hear the dog barking!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No that's just me coughing! Oh, good, there you are! Listen, um, I have a dog in my room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is it doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm watching it for some friends who went out of town. Wait. Hello, my name is Clunkers. May I please stay with you nice people?
lines.stage_direction: She bends down, picks up the dog, and waves with one of its paws

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oooh, I wish she could stay here, but Chandler is allergic!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Extremely allergic, okay? If I'm anywhere near a dog for more than 5 minutes, my throat will just close up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's odd, 'cause this dog's been living here for the past 3 days

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, if that dog's been here that long, and you haven't had a reaction, maybe you're not allergic to this dog?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it still has to go, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's um…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't do it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't do what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have to! Okay? It's time! Okay, I hate dogs.
lines.stage_direction: to Joey Joey shrugs as if to say, "Do what you have to do"

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you crazy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you out of your mind?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Told ya.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They are needy, they are jumpy, and you can't tell what they are thinking, and that scares me a little bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, they are scary. Ahh, she just ate a treat out of my hand!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute. Do you not like all dogs? I mean, not even puppies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is there a puppy here?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: You don't like puppies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, you are new!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Chandler, I told you, never tell anyone about this dog thing. It's like Ross not likin’ ice cream.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't like ice cream?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's too cold.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's just that dogs make me a little uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It hurts my teeth.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I don't wanna say this, I don't you guys to hate me, but uh, I don't think, I can be around that dog anymore. Okay, so either the dog goes, or I go. Oh my god!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How can I not get this? I'm a college professor; I got 1450 on my S.A.T.s.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damn, I forgot you were here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. We're gonna take Clunkers to Ross’s. We'll be back in a minute.
lines.stage_direction: Gets up with Monica to do so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, wait before you guys go, can I just ask you a question?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When a guy breaks up with his girlfriend, what is an appropriate amount of time to wait before you make a move?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'd say about a month.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? I'd say 3 to 4.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Half hour.
lines.stage_direction: Rachel turns to look at him and he nods yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When it's your assistant, I would say never.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, Rach, the big question is, does he like you? All right? Because if he doesn't like you, this is all a moo-point.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. A moo-point?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's like a cow's opinion. It just doesn't matter. It's moo.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Have I been living with him for too long, or did that all just make sense?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please, don't listen to Joey, okay. Would you look at him? He-he’s obviously depressed. He's away from his family; he's spending Thanksgiving with strangers. What he needs right now is for you to be his friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're right, I'm sorry. Thank you. Okay, that's what I'm gonna do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine! Take their advice. No one ever listens to me. When the package is this pretty, no one cares what's inside.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Not bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah? I'm sorry about your girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So were you guys together a long time?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: A year. On and off. I kinda thought we'd end up together. I don't anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now that she broke up with you?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Hmmmm.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It's weird. I always used to assume, that I would meet someone and fall in love and be happy and all that was just a given. But lately it's like what if it's not. Do you ever have that feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No…Yeah, all the time, constantly. It's terrifying. But you know that I figure it…it has to work out.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, uh—it has to.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: You have all the answers, don't you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I do. I really do.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey, thanks for talking to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what is a boss for? Hug it out!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, he likes you back! Huh? Told ya, you should go for it!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Street noise drowned any of that out? No, all right, I see you later, okay...
lines.stage_direction: realizes what he said Rachel moves madly towards him Turns away embarrassed

lines.text: What did Joey say? I like you back?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, yeah, well, see, he… Joey knows, that I'm-I'm very insecure about my back and, and…you're hugging me, so obviously you are not repulsed by it, yeah!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Wait-wait a minute; that doesn't make any sense.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No? All right, here's the truth um, Joey said what he said, because um, I'm attracted to you.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I admit it. I have a crush on you, and uh, and, and I know that's crazy because we work together, and-and nothing could ever happen, and the last thing I want to do is-is to freak you out or make you feel uncomfortable. Which is why it would be really great if you said something right about now.

lines.text: Oh my god! Those guys are stealing my car!
lines.stage_direction: looking at the street He points down to the street

lines.text: What?

lines.text: Right there! That's my car! Hey!!

lines.text: Okay, that's gonna take them a minute. Do you have anything else you wanna get off your chest?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, we still have time to talk and they’re-they're not even in the car yet! Oh look, there they go, okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on. All right, here we go. All right?
lines.stage_direction: She gets on the bike. They start.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. Feel good?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, try pedaling.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s it, your doing great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Doing great! Yes-yes-yes! Take control! Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Weee!!!
lines.stage_direction: Ross pushes her and lets go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!! Yes!!!
lines.stage_direction: He starts clapping and Phoebe turns around to look at him and falls off the bike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Oh! Oh no!! You swore!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I just thought you were doing so well. I…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am shocked! Shocked!!
lines.stage_direction: She storms off leaving Ross to defend himself from the angry looks from onlookers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s a legitimate learning technique. Wow!
lines.stage_direction: Looking around and seeing the woman behind him glaring at him.

scenes.scene_description: Central Park, Ross is teaching Phoebe how to ride her bike.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, there’s uh, some people outside, askin’ about candy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, they’re just gonna have to wait aren’t they? I’ve only got two hands!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Need some help?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! You don’t know the system! There’ll be nobody messing with the system!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: By the way, the week before your wedding you may not see a lot of me.
lines.stage_direction: She glares at him and he quickly makes his way to Phoebe’s room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hello liar.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I-I’m really sorry I let go of the bike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I could’ve been killed I hope you know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. I know. But, can we please try it again? Huh? I mean, you were so close Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I would love to but…the bike got stolen and the police have no suspects.
lines.stage_direction: Ross just happens to have his hand on a sheet that is covering something that suspiciously looks like a bike.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! What the hell?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, y’know what? If you are not going to learn how to ride this bike then I’m sorry, I’m just gonna have to take it back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because! Because, it-it-it’s… It’d be like you having this guitar and-and never playing it. Okay, this guitar wants to be played! And-and this bike wants to be ridden and-and if you don’t ride it you-you’re-you’re killing its spirit! The bike is dying.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. If you care enough to make up that load of crap, okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Great! You’re making the bike very happy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay Ross! Please don’t die!
lines.stage_direction: sarcastically Ross exits and she says quietly to the bike

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is making candy like mad as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I think we can get the evaluation back before they see it, but we’re gonna have to get into Mr. Zelner’s office. Now, he doesn’t get in until 10, so he’s no problem, but his assistant, Betty, she comes in early to eat her breakfast at her desk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: That’s kinda sad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well Betty’s kinda sad. Which is why I believe I can lure her away with these chocolates. Now, while I distract her, you get in the office.
lines.stage_direction: Holding up the box of chocolates.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Got it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let’s roll!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s office, Tag and her are planning how to get the review back.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Hello Rachel, you uh, got a minute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure Mr. Zelner, for you anything—minute. Okay. Fine. Abort the plan, abort the plan.
lines.stage_direction: worried and shocked To Tag She start to usher Tag out.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Uh actually, I’d like to speak with both of you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Uh, well can we, can we get you anything Mr. Zelner? Maybe some chocolates?

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Umm, no. Thanks, but I’ll give these to Betty. So I read your evaluation of Tag, or to use his full name, Tag Sweetcheeks Jones. Is something going on with you two?
lines.stage_direction: Rachel glances at Tag to say, "See?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. Can you imagine if there was?! I mean, what would happen exactly.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Well, I’d be forced to file a report. I’d have to consult with the legal department, and your future at the company would be in jeopardy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well…

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Uh Mr. Zelner, I’m the one who filled in that evaluation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no…

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah-yeah, I thought it would be funny.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: You wrote that you have a cute touchie?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yes. I have a weird sense of humor, and I’m kinda strangely proud of my butt.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: It’s kind of a risky joke Tag, and what is-what is this drawing I can’t figure out what this is?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You’re lookin’ at it upside down—y’know what? It doesn’t matter.
lines.stage_direction: Grabs the evaluation and throws it out.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Yeah, it’s not like I don’t have a sense of humor, huh? Hell, I even enjoy a naughty limerick now and then. But there’s a time and a place, huh?! Unless you uh, have a limerick right now? No? Okay, well uh, you’ve got my fax number.
lines.stage_direction: They both nod no. Grabs the chocolates. Exits.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa! I can’t believe you did that. That was really sweet.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No, don’t worry about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, you could’ve lost your job.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Are you kidding me?! With a cute butt like this, I’d find work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! You’re great!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Y’know what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I feel great. In fact! What?
lines.stage_direction: Rachel laughs Walks over to her desk, sweeps its contents onto the floor and Rachel just glares at him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I… It just—it took me so long to get that desk organized.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry.
lines.stage_direction: He bends over to pick the stuff up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There it is.
lines.stage_direction: looking at his butt They hug.

scenes.scene_description: They start towards the door but are stopped when Mr. Zelner enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is going on?
lines.stage_direction: asking a man leaning against the wall

lines.speaker: The Man
lines.text: We’re waiting for the candy. Bring out the candy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah lady! Give us candy!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What’s up buddy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Waiting for candy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Get in here! Hey, and you can not smoke in here! Merry Christmas.
lines.stage_direction: They head for the door and Chandler sees Smokes-A-Lot Lady standing next to the door and smoking, to her Takes the cigarette and takes a drag for himself. Exhaling in ecstasy

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, guys! The candy is coming; I just need another 15 minutes for the chocolate to cool!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: We want candy! We want candy now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right everybody! Just be quiet! Be quiet! Be quiet!! Pipe-pipe-pipe down! What is the matter with you people?! This woman was trying to do a nice thing for you. She was making candy so she could try to get to know all of you, and I’ll bet that not one of you can tell me her name! Am I right?

lines.speaker: The Man
lines.text: Candy Lady?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No not Candy Lady.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, if we know it can we have candy?!!
lines.stage_direction: stepping in and knocking the man over

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, y’know what? Forget it, all of you forget it! You’ve ruined it! Go home! You’ve ruined it! You’ve ruined it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s right, it’s all ruined! You guys ruined everything! You ruined it!
lines.stage_direction: Steps into the apartment and Chandler closes the door. Joey struts over to the candy and starts eating it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re welcome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you smoke?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Smokes-A-Lot Lady blew smoke directly into my mouth. Eh-uh—are you okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m fine now, but it was really scary there for a while. I mean, someone slipped a-a threatening note under the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, sorry about that. Mob mentality or whatever, I don’t know…
lines.stage_direction: with his mouth full Grabs the note.

scenes.scene_description: The Hallway, Chandler is returning from work to see the hallway jammed full of people waiting outside their door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe it! I did it! I rode a bike! I never thought I’d be able to do that! Thank you Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey, don’t thank me, thank yourself. You’re the one who faced her fears and ultimately overcame them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t be so corny Ross, it’s not an after-school special.
lines.stage_direction: She rides off and the camera pans down to reveal two shiny, silver training wheels firmly attached.

scenes.scene_description: Central Park, Phoebe is now riding her bike with ease and stops in front of Ross. They both giggle.

title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Buh-bye. I just got us reservations at Michelle’s and tickets to the Musicman to celebrate our first holiday season as a betroughed couple.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Betrothed…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: …betrothed couple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Haaaa... ... ahhhh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Skull?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, it's my mom's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my god!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no. It's not! It's not my mom. It belonged to mom. Yeah, no, she used to put it out every Christmas to remind us, that even though it's Christmas, people still die. And, you can put candy in it.
lines.stage_direction: She grabs the skull, pulls out a stick of licorice, and takes a bite.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Licorice?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Hey, I just found out, I get Ben for the holidays this year.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Ohh! That's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you gonna dress up as Santa?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nope. I mean, I know Susan does every year, but I think I wanna take this year to teach him all about Hanukkah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And maybe I could teach Ben about the Christmas skull and how people die.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You may need to use this year to teach Ben about Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you know he was in there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How long have we been home?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: About a half an hour.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Love ly!

scenes.scene_description: Joey comes out of the bathroom reading a newspaper

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what I was thinking? When we get married, are you gonna change your last name to Bing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bing's weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Guess what! My landlord just called and my apartment is gonna get ready soon, so I guess I'll be moving out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ahh, Phoebe, I'm gonna miss you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, you will be very sad. All right, well I gotta go tell Rachel the good news.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh! You guys gonna be living together again?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she's just so much fun with Joey, I just assumed, she'd still be living with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why do you think, she's having so much fun living with Joey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No reason, except…she…told…me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really? So she said, she didn’t wanna live with me anymore?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No, she didn't say that. I-I-I think you should talk to Monica now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, don't worry about it. I'm sure she wants to live with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're sure? You're absolutely sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, no. But, um, I bet she probably does.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Probably? Yeah, I don't like that word. Kind of what ‘probably’ really means. Yeah, uh-huh. Yeah, oh, "Your mom probably won't kill herself," y’know? I'm sorry, but I'm not hanging all my hopes of Rachel and I living together on-on "Probably!" Y’know? You gotta take care of yourselves! In this world history teaches us nothing!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler and Monica look surprised She starts to walk out Exits

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bing doesn't seem so weird now, does it?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler is getting a cup of coffee and sits down next to Monica.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Great, you’re home! Guess what Phoebe got me for Christmas!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel’s, Joey is sitting behind a red drum set.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Drums?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Drums!

scenes.scene_description: Starts drumming.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, could we get two burritos to go, please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry. But not that sorry, 'cause you don't have to live with it. Um, we have a reservation under the name Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Oh-kay, we'll have a table for you in about 45 minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Forty-five minutes? We have tickets to the Musicman

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: I'm sorry. Christmas is a very busy time, sir.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is this because of the burrito thing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You need to give him money.
lines.stage_direction: pulling Chandler away from the Maitre d'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Give him money? It was a joke!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, to get a table! Places like are always shakin’ you down. Everybody wants to be paid off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right, calm down, O'Mally. I'll slip him some money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You've got to be smooth about it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I can be smooth. Listen, we're a little bit in a hurry, so, if you can get us a table a little quicker, I'd appreciate it.
lines.stage_direction: Walks back to the Maitre d', very smoothly Shakes his hand

lines.speaker: Maitre d'
lines.text: Of course, sir.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How did it go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Had the money in the wrong hand.

lines.text: Wooooooo, hehehe. Hey, ahh, you don't feel like you're gonna throw up, do ya?

lines.text: No.

lines.text: Well, I do, so let's... So, Ben, you uh, you know what holiday is coming up, don't ya?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Christmas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep, and you know what other holiday is coming up?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Christmas eve.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, but also Hanukkah! See, you're part Jewish, and-and Hanukkah is a Jewish holiday.
lines.stage_direction: Pauses to let Ben answer, but he doesn’t.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa has reindeers that can fly!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, um, but, on Hanukkah, Hanukkah is a celebration of a miracle. See, years and years ago there were these people called the Maccabees.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Jingle bells, jingle bells ...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, that's right, yes, but on Hanukkah, uh, we sing, uh Dreidel, dreidel, dreidel, I made you out of clay .

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Rudolph, the red-nosed reindeer ...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it's not a contest.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: When is Santa coming?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, how about this year, instead of Santa, we have fun celebrating Hanukkah?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No Santa? Was I bad?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! Oh, no-no-no. Hey, you weren't bad, you've been very good, Ben.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa's mad at me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, hey-hey, come on, Ben, Santa is not mad at you, okay? Hey, you're-you're his favorite little guy!
lines.stage_direction: He grabs Ben and sits him on his lap

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: So Santa's coming?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! Santa's coming!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's easy! Just keep it casual! Give him a kind word, shake his hand and give him the money!

lines.text: How do you know so much about this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Richard used to do it, didn't he?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We'd be eating our soup right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mustached bastard…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, those people just left, come on! Quick! Give him the money and get their table!

lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.text: Good evening.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahh-hahaha!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ha!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you like the drums! That's, that's great! Y’know, I was worried, that, you know, they would maybe an unbearable living situation. All right, okay, well, apparently not! So, yay!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, Pheebs, check it out, we already learned a song. Ready? One, two, three, four...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's fun.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you Gunther, put it there. Definitely not easier with coins. Thank you.
lines.stage_direction: He gets up, and shakes Gunther's hand. A bunch of coins fall out his hand. He sits down next to Monica. Joey gets up and picks up the coins. Chandler thinks, Joey is just helping him to pick them up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you. Hey Pheebs!
lines.stage_direction: He gets up and puts on his jacket. Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Here. Now I only owe you $49.50.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!
lines.stage_direction: she sits down next to him

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you wanna give Joey a Christmas present that disrupts the entire building, why not get him something a little bit more subtle, like a wrecking ball, or a vile of small pox to release in the hallway?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's not just the drum noise. Every five minutes, Joey throws his sticks in the air, and I have to hear, "Oh my eye! Oh god, my eye!" I mean, it is so annoying.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, thank you. You see, this is how normal people are supposed to react to drums.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you got Joey drums to annoy Rachel, so she wouldn't wanna live there anymore?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Maybe on some level.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, y’know that you could just not throw the sticks up in the air.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is Rock 'n' Roll about that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Joey, I got you another present.
lines.stage_direction: entering with an aquarium covered by a towel She puts it on the counter

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh wait, before you tell me what it is! Okay, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a…tarantula! Oh! God! Rachel, look, I'm sorry. What was I thinking giving Joey this big, gross, scary spider in such a poorly constructed cage?
