lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you talking about? I love them! Yeah, I had a tarantula when I was a kid. But it-it died, because my cat ate it. And then, then my cat died. But Joey, isn't this cool?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it on me? I feel, I feel like it's on me! I got, hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, isn't that adorable? Joey is afraid of the tarantula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah, yeah, he's so adorable, God, he's just so much fun, Joey is the best, I'm glad you're having so much fun here.
lines.stage_direction: sarcastic She turns around, about to leave

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wait-wait a minute, what? Phoebe, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Our apartment is ready.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And that makes you angry because…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because you would rather live here with Joey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where did you get that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica and Chandler said that you were having so much fun here. And apparently no amount of drums or tarantulas is gonna change that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Did you get all this stuff for Joey to try and drive me out of the apartment? Honey, if you wanted to do that, you might as well just gotten him a fish, you know how fish freaked me out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fish!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It wouldn't have mattered anyway, Phoebe, you and I are, are gonna live together, we're roommates; that's the deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, but I wanted you to want to live with me, but okay, if you're having so much fun over here…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it's so much more fun with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We did have fun, didn't we?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We did!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, anyway, they say, if we want, we can see it tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I would love to!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay, okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good, good, good, good, good.
lines.stage_direction: She runs to the drums and gets the sticks

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great, all right, okay, and Monica ask me to make the drumming stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um... Done!
lines.stage_direction: with the sticks in her hands She goes to the tarantula-cage and puts the sticks into it

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is sitting behind the drums wearing safety goggles, hitting them with his sticks as Rachel watches.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Hello, Sir. You're here to return those pants?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, these are my pants.

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Oh. Okay! How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, uh, do you have a Santa-outfit left?

lines.speaker: Salesman
lines.text: Two days before Christmas? Sorry, man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay look, do-do, you have anything Christmassy? I promised my son, and I really don't want to disappoint him, um, come on, I…uh, you gotta have something.

scenes.scene_description: Halloween Adventure , a costume shop, there is a salesman behind the counter, Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm the holiday armadillo! I'm a friend of Santa's and he sent me here to wish you a Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened to Santa, Holiday Armadillo?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Santa was unavailable so close to Christmas.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, come in, have a seat. You must be exhausted coming all the way from…Texas.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Texas?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right, Ben. I'm Santa's representative for all the southern states. And Mexico! But, Santa sent me here to give you these presents, Ben. Maybe the Lady will help me with these presents.
lines.stage_direction: He tries to bend down to pick up the bag with the presents, but can't because of the costume

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Wow! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're welcome, Ben. Merry Christmas, ooh, and Happy Hanukkah!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Are you for Hanukkah, too? Because I'm part-Jewish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You are? Me, too!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because Armadillos also wandered in the desert?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You wanna wander in the hall? Ooh, hey Ben, what if the Holiday Armadillo told you all about the festival of lights?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Cool!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Ben.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Years and years ago there were these people called the Maccabees...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ho, ho, ho! Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Santa!
lines.stage_direction: Runs to Chandler and hugs him

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!
lines.stage_direction: Grunts as Ben hits him at full speed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing here, Santa?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm here to see my old buddy Ben. What are you doing here, weird…turtle-man?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm the Holiday Armadillo, your part-Jewish friend. You sent me here to give Ben some presents. Remember?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Did you bring me any presents, Santa?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You bet I did, Ben, put it there! Well, it would have worked this time, if his hands weren't so damn small! Ho, ho, ho!
lines.stage_direction: He shakes Ben's hand, but the money falls out of his hands to Monica Realizes, that Ben is standing right there

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay Ben, why don't you come open some more presents, and Santa, the Armadillo, and I have a little talk in the kitchen? There's a sentence, I never thought I'd say.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You called everyone and said you were having trouble finding a Santa costume, so I borrowed one from a guy at work!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, but, but you gotta leave.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because, I'm finally getting him excited about Hanukkah, and, and you're-you’re wrecking it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But I didn't get to shape my belly like a bowl full of jelly.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, Chandler but this, this is really important to me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine, I'll give the suit back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, you think, you can keep it another night?
lines.stage_direction: to Chandler She has a really teasing look on her face and keeps twirling Chandler's beard.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Santa? Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, is that okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did your Dad ever dress up like Santa?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then it's okay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay Ben, Santa has to go. Say good-bye!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No! Why does he have to go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because, if Santa and the Holiday…Armadillo? ...are ever in the same room for too long the universe will implode. Merry Christmas!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: No! Why can't the Armadillo leave? I want Santa!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, I-I give up. Santa, Santa can stay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'll stay, but only because I wanna hear about Hanukkah. Ben, will you sit here with Santa and learn about Hanukkah?

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Okay, Santa!

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica has just opened the door for Ross who is costumed as an Armadillo. Ben is standing next to her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, it's time for the story of Hanukkah. Years and years ago, there were these people called the Maccabees.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Merry Christmas!

scenes.scene_description: Ross mouths to Chandler, "Thank you," and he mouths, "You’re welcome," back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! Look at this place!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this is terrible. Oh, they’ve made so many changes I can’t even feel my grandmother’s presence anymore—Ooh! New sconces!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, remember uh, remember how you told me that your grandmother put up that wall to make that into two bedrooms?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And remember how you always said you were afraid the landlord would find out and then tear it down?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you really not know where I’m going with this? It left! It’s one huge room!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! Oh! Wow!!!
lines.stage_direction: She runs to see. Running back, excitedly

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: See?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I guess we’ll just have to put the wall back up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can’t, because of the new skylight!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There’s a skylight?! Wow!!
lines.stage_direction: Runs to see and yells from the bedroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what should we do? Should we start looking for a new place?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know I’m-I’m sensing that um, my grandmother would not be comfortable with that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Startin’ to feel her again there are we?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little bit, yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs is your grandmother maybe saying that you should live here alone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You heard her too?! You have the gift!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, it’s okay. I like living with Joey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, I hate packing, it’s closer to work, and we do have fun. Although, I’m really gonna miss living with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. Oh-oh, wait did you hear that-hear that? Listen, I’m gettin’ something from your grandmother, she said that since you get to keep the one bedroom apartment you should give Rachel the purple chair?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I do not hear that.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe and Rachel's, they are entering to check out the newly refurbished apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: …and the miracle was that that little bit of oil that should’ve just lasted just one day, burned for…

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Eight whole days.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s right, and that’s why we celebrate Hanukkah today. The end.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.text: Awesome!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My favorite part was when Superman flew all the Jews out of Egypt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The Armadillo was actually not so thrilled about that part! Okay Ben, it’s time to light the Hanukkah candles!
lines.stage_direction: Santa, Superman , Monica, Ben, and the Holiday Armadillo go over to the menorah to light the candles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! It looks like the Easter Bunny’s funeral in here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, come on, we’re-we’re-we’re lighting the candles!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Santa (Chandler), Superman (Joey), Ben, and Monica are listening to the Holiday Armadillo (Ross) finish telling the story of Hanukkah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I understand why Superman is here, but why is there a porcupine at the Easter Bunny’s funeral?

scenes.scene_description: They both go over to light the candles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it back in the cage?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Its back in cage!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cage closed?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, would you just come out here and stop being such a baby!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is closing the door on the tarantula cage.
Joey throws open his door and stands there still in the Superman costume then slowly makes his way to the bathroom while keeping both eyes on the cage.

title: The One With All The Cheesecake

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh. Mmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.
lines.stage_direction: Comes in the front door and walks towards the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you have got to try this cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y’know I'm not that much of a sweet tooth. I——Wow. My God, so creamy. Oh my God, this is the best cheesecake I have ever had. Where did you get this?
lines.stage_direction: Chandler puts a forkful of the cheesecake in her mouth. She reaches over to look at the label on the box.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was at the front door. When I got home. Somebody sent it to us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, this is not addressed to you. This is addressed to Mrs. Braverman downstairs. Thief.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I—no! I didn't read the box before I opened it. And you can't return a box after you've opened the box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why, why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because it's too delicious.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, you stole this cheesecake. That is wrong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no! It is going to be okay, because Mrs. Braverman is gonna send away for a free one and that way we all win! The only losers are the big cheesecake conglomerate, Momma’s Little Bakery. I feel terrible, I’m a horrible, horrible, horrible person.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I’m sorry what?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Monica’s, Chandler is sitting at the kitchen table eating cheesecake. The box it came in is also on the table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I should get going, big day a work. Y’know I’m in a coma? Today, they do this test on me and it turns out I’m not brain dead.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah-ah-ah Mr. Smartie Pants, it’s just not my character that’s not brain dead. Hey, so Pheebs, we still on for tonight?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ll see you at 8:00.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, what’s at 8:00?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I have dinner plans with Joey. We get together about once a month to discuss the rest of you guys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, did not know that! May I say how lovely you look today?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Duly noted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. Oh! So for tomorrow, do you want to rent a car and drive down together or what?
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe gets up to get a refill. To Monica

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cousin Frannie’s wedding, its tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You were invited?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My God, I can’t believe this! I mean I knew that mom and dad were invited, but I thought that was it! I mean from the ages 7 to 9 Frannie and I were inseparable!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, maybe since the age of 9, Frannie’s made some new friends.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well l-look okay, it’s probably just a mistake. Let me call Aunt Sheryl okay? Maybe you are invited and the invitation just got lost in the mail.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, well you call her and tell her that y’know when we were kids her precious little Frannie tried to undress me several times, okay? And if I hadn’t have stopped her, there probably wouldn’t even be a wedding to go too.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know, she tried to undress me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I used to undress my cousin Glenn.
lines.stage_direction: Monica looks at him then sushes him.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, everyone except Rachel is there as Joey gets up and starts putting on his coat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joseph Francis Tribbiani are you home yet?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, I think he’s still out. What’s wrong?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I’ll tell you Rachel Karen Green, I had plans with Joey tonight and he left me this note.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "Pheebs, can’t make it, got a date. Talk to you later. Big Daddy." Big Daddy?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that’s a nickname we were trying out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y’know what nickname never caught on? The Ross-A-Tron!
lines.stage_direction: Monica shakes her head in disgust.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Here’s Joseph Francis!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-Wha-Ho! What are you middle naming me for?! I left you a note!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what?! That doesn’t give you the right to ditch me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you can cancel plans with friends if there is the possibility for sex!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe he’s right, that is the rule.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t accept this rule. When me make plans, I expect you to show up. Okay, I can’t just be a way to kill time ‘til you meet someone better! Y’know boyfriends and girlfriends come and go, but this is for life!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! I’m so sorry; I had no idea it would bother you this much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it does.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, can I-can I make it up to you? Huh? I’m sorry. How about uh, dinner tomorrow night?! I’ll pay for myself!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, you wore me down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Joe, while you’re over there how about another beer for the Ross-A-Tron?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Ross—Is that back?!!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Monica, Chandler, Rachel, and Ross are playing Monopoly as Phoebe enters angrily.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Another cheesecake came! They delivered it to the wrong address again!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So just bring it back downstairs, what’s the problem?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can’t seem to say goodbye.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you serious?! Chandler, we ate an entire cheesecake two days ago and you want more?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I’ve forgotten what it tastes like okay?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It was cheesecake. It was fine. It had a buttery, crumbly, graham cracker crust, with a very rich yet light, cream cheese filling… Wow! My whole mouth just filled with saliva!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what? Forget it! We are just hungry! We have not had lunch! We are just light-headed! So let us go out and have lunch and forget about the cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah and we’ll drop it off downstairs so that we’re not tempted.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Good idea. Where do you want to go to lunch?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Momma’s Little Bakery, Chicago, Illinois.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Rachel enters to find Chandler staring at another cheesecake box.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Awww! Mel Torme died.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, that paper’s like a year old!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aw! Does that mean the Sam Goody’s sale is over?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I finally heard back from Aunt Sheryl and apparently it wasn’t a mistake. Ahh, there’s-there’s limited seating in the hall.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Limited seating?! I am just one tiny person!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well yeah, but she doesn’t know that. I mean, the last time she saw you-you would’ve turned one of those little wedding chairs into kindling.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Limited seating! Oh, that is such a lame excuse! That’s not the reason she’s not inviting me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh what’s the big deal?! I wasn’t even invited to the ceremony, just the reception. And-and y’know what? If it makes you feel any better, Joan and I will just make an appearance and then, and then we’ll-we’ll leave early as a sign of protest.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joan?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, Joan Tedeski my date. She’s an assistant professor in the Linguistics department. Tall, very beautiful, and despite what some people say, not broad backed!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, you got Ross Gellar and guest?! I wasn’t invited and you got "and guest?!"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh-uh, excuse me, I do have to interrupt on Ross’s behalf. I-I think the rule applies here y’know, since she has a chance to get on broad back…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, y’know, you’re bringing me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? I can’t cancel on Joan!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bec—Did you not hear me?! She’s an assistant professor in the Linguistics department, okay? They’re wild! Why do you want to come anyway?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because! She’s my cousin. I mean, we grew up together! We’re family y’know? Well that’s important to me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, all right, I’ll take you. I’ll go call Joan.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww that’s nice. Family should be there, huh? This is her wedding, happiest day of her life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We’ll see.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Monica and Joey are there. Joey is reading a newspaper.

scenes.location: The Lobby of Chandler and Rachel’s building

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, thank you for lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Wait a minute, I didn’t pay, I thought you paid!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So apparently we just don’t pay for food anymore. Do you see what I see?
lines.stage_direction: Rachel laughs then Chandler notices something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Its still there!
lines.stage_direction: gasps The cheesecake they returned to Mrs. Braverman is still lying in front of her door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mrs. Braverman must be out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She could be out of town. Maybe she’ll be gone for months.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: By then, the cheesecake may have gone bad. We don’t want her to come back to bad cheesecake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No that could kill her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we don’t want that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No so we’re protecting her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we should take it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But we should move quick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because I think I just heard her moving around in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go! Go!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel grabs the cheesecake and they take off upstairs.

scenes.scene_description: The Lobby of Chandler and Rachel’s building, Chandler and Rachel are returning from lunch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! That’s David!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: David who?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David the scientist guy, David that I was in love with, David who went to Russia and broke my heart David!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you say someone’s name enough, they turn around.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: David! What-what are you doing here? Aren’t you supposed to be in Russia?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I’m just, I’m just in town for a conference. Umm, God you look phenomenal!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well… Yeah. You look great too. Did you get a haircut?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah. Well I-I got like thirty of them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Umm, look I-I-I got a confession to make…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh, I-I-I was hoping to run into you here. I didn’t know whether I should call or not, y’know I-I was only in town for a few days. And y’know, I didn’t want to intrude on your life or-or anything like that, but I-I really wanted to see you and—but I didn’t know if you wanted to see me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, of course I would want to see you. I…I think about you all the time.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Really? Because I think about you all the time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I mean, there’s a statue in Minsk…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: That reminds me of you so much, I mean umm, it-it’s actually of Lenin. But, y’know at certain angles…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Umm, anyway…Do you want to have dinner tonight?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! Oh no!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh. Uh, what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t. I can’t believe I have plans, I can’t. Can you do it tomorrow night though?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Uh no, I have to go in a few hours. I have to be on the red-eye. Well listen, y’know, next time you’re in Minsk umm…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, can I talk to you for a second?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I have plans with Joey tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So! He’ll understand!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No he won’t. And that’s not even the point! Monica, I made a whole speech about you do not cancel plans with friends! And now y’know what? Just because, potentially, the love of my life comes back from Russia just for one night, I-I should change my beliefs?! I should change beliefs! No! No! No, if I don’t have my principles, I don’t have anything!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, you are so strong.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or! I should rush through dinner with Joey and I can meet David at 9:00!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Monica and Phoebe are entering. As Phoebe is sitting down, she recognizes someone sitting at the counter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! That is so good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m full, and yet I know if I stop eating this, I’ll regret it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What do you got there?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it’s umm, it’s tofu cake. Do you want some?
lines.stage_direction: He makes a disgusted noise and heads for his room, Chandler follows him in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh? Uh…
lines.stage_direction: He starts taking off his pants.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dude! Dude!
lines.stage_direction: Motions that Joey should pull up his pants.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Sorry. Uh, I’ve got those plans with Phoebe, why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh really? Uh, Monica said she had a date at 9:00.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Tonight?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s what Monica said.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: After she gave me that big speech?! She goes and makes a date with a guy on the same night she has plans with me? I think she’s trying to pull a fast one on Big Daddy!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Chandler are eating the cheesecake right out of the box.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh here, I think this is us. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Limited seating my ass. Let’s see who made the cut. Hi!
lines.stage_direction: To the couple sitting to her right.

lines.speaker: The Wedding Guest
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m Monica Gellar. Who do you know the bride and groom?

lines.speaker: The Wedding Guest
lines.text: Oh, I used to work with Frannie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Used to work with her. Used to! I’m a relative and I didn’t get invited! A blood relative! Blood!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Stop saying, "Blood" to strangers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, how about you huh? How do you know the happy couple?
lines.stage_direction: to the couple on her left

lines.speaker: The Second Guest
lines.text: We went to college with both of them and now we live next door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you’re fine.

scenes.scene_description: Cousin Frannie’s Wedding Reception, Monica and Ross are entering and finding their table.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Are you guys ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! I will have the green salad, umm the house salad, and water’s fine.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: And for you sir?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, this slow roasted salmon, just how slow are we talkin’ here?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: It’s uh, it’s already been roasted.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, then no. Maybe I should hear those specials again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Joey, we’ve heard the specials three times! Okay? There’s prime rib, mahi mahi, and a very special lobster ravioli.
lines.stage_direction: She grabs his menu and hands it to the waiter.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Actually we’re out of the lobster ravioli.
lines.stage_direction: Putting Joey’s menu under his arm.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, that changes everything! Y’know what Pheebs?
lines.stage_direction: Grabs his menu and starts looking at it again. The waiter leaves.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You were right before. I mean, friends are so important.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I’m very wise. I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know-y’know what I really want?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is to have a long, long talk. Y’know? Get Joey out on the open road and really open him up.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Any progress?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! I will have the lobster ravioli.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God Joey, this is taking forever!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What’s the rush? What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: W-w—I just—it’s that—I have—y’know I have-I have an appointment. And it’s very important.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well… It’s a date.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A date?! No, no Pheebs you-you must be mistaken, because I know you wouldn’t schedule a date on the same night you have plans with a friend!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on Joey, don’t make me feel badly about this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I’m gonna!! That’s right! Yeah, you made me feel really guilty about goin’ out with that girl! Like-like-like I did something terrible to you! And now Pheebs, you’re doing the same thing!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That—It’s not the same thing! This is totally different! This is with David! Remember David, the scientist guy? Okay, he’s very special to me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, well my girl from the other night was special. She was a scientist too!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She was?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, she graduated from high school!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, whatever. Y’know what? I don’t have time have time to convince you because he’s only here for four hours, and I’m gonna go see him!

scenes.scene_description: Iridium Restaurant, Phoebe and Joey are looking at the menus as the waiter comes to take their order.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you still doin’ here?! I told you, lobster ravioli!

scenes.scene_description: Gets up and leaves.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Hey! Oh, I was just about to leave. I-I-I-I didn’t think you were coming.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I wouldn’t miss this.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Well, I’m very glad you’re here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you’re such a gentleman. Come on! We’re going to my place!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs his arm. Drags him off to her place.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Central Perk, Phoebe is running up to meet David.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you eating the cheesecake without me?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Mm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I will give you a hundred dollars to whistle right now. How can you eat the cheesecake without me?!
lines.stage_direction: She tries to whistle and blows little chunks of cheesecake out of her mouth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, what are you going to do?! Are you gonna go run tell Monica?! Are you gonna tell Joey?! No! Because then you will have to tell them what we did! We are desert stealers! We are living outside the law!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what? I don’t trust you with this cake anymore! And I got it first, and I’m takin’ it back!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs the cheesecake and heads for his apartment.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no-no, no you don’t!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes! Oh yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You think I trust you with it?! No! We’re gonna split it! You take half and I take half!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that’s not fair, you’ve already had some!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Oh, well then y’know what? I think Monica would be very interested to know that you called her cheesecake dry and mealy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do we use to split it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! All right, pick a half.
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a knife and cuts it in half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay well, this side looks bigger. Uh… There’s more crust on this side. Y’know? So, maybe if I measured…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh for God sake just pick a piece!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, I’ll pick that one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s also the smaller piece. Okay, there you go. Enjoy your half my friend, but that is it. No sharing. No switching, and don’t come crying to me if you eat your piece to fast. Oh!!!!
lines.stage_direction: Puts the piece onto a plate. As she’s saying that she is backing out the door, when she finishes she turns around to return to her place, stumbles and drops the cheesecake on the floor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohhh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you gotta give me some of your piece.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho-ho-no! No! No switching! No sharing, and don’t come crying to me! Ha-ha-ha! I may just sit here and have my cake all day! Just sit here in the hallway and eat my…
lines.stage_direction: Rachel knocks the plate from his hand and it falls on the floor. That process leaves just the forkful Chandler has, Rachel starts to go after that little bit and Chandler retreats into his apartment.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is eating the cheesecake and Chandler enters and catches her in the act.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, hey Aunt Millie.

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Isn’t it a beautiful wedding?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, yes it is. It’s uh… Every time on the lips! Why?! Why on lips?!
lines.stage_direction: Aunt Millie uses this opportunity to grab Ross and kiss him on the lips. After she leaves Ross quickly wipes his mouth with a napkin.

scenes.scene_description: Cousin Frannie’s Wedding Reception, Monica and Ross are sitting at the table, alone as a woman approaches.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here’s Frannie. Hmm, won’t she be happy to see me?
lines.stage_direction: Starts to get up and great Frannie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now wait a minute, you be nice! All right? I didn’t bring you here so you can ambush her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Frannie was the one who found your Playboys and showed them to mom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That bitch!
lines.stage_direction: He gets up and they go over to ambush Frannie. Monica taps on Frannie’s shoulder.

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.text: Monica! What…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Am I doing here? Why? Surprised to see me? Ross brought me. How do you like that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Frannie, congratulations.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You invite my brother, you invite my whole family, and not me?! Why?! What—Why wouldn’t you want me at your wedding? What could I have possibly done?! Stuart!

lines.speaker: Frannie
lines.text: I believe you know my husband.

scenes.scene_description: Cousin Frannie and her husband enter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So it’s really a question of who could you have possibly done.

scenes.scene_description: Monica is shocked into silence.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Oh, I hate this but I-I-I have to go. I-I can’t miss my flight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you sure? I’ll bet there’s another flight to Minsk in like…

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: July. Umm,

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s really beautiful. What does it mean?

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Please, clean my beakers. I don’t get out of the lab much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s good. I got to admit, I thought it was something else.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Yeah, I… Well I really actually wanted to say umm, that, but um, I figured I probably shouldn’t because y’know, I…have to leave.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re right! You’re right. Don’t say it.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: I-I do though.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do too.

lines.speaker: David
lines.text: Bye Phoebe.
lines.stage_direction: He gets in and the cab drives off. Joey walks up and witnesses that event.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, now’s not the time Joey. All right? You can yell at me tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No! No Pheebs, I’m not gonna yell at you. I just y’know, started thinking about you and David and I…remember how bummed you were the first time he left. And I just… Oh Pheebs, come here. Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I’m not okay. The only guy I’ve ever been crazy about has gone to Minsk and I may never…I may never see him again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y’know you could always visit him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh right, like they’re gonna let me have a passport.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anything I can do? Whatever you need.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well—But—Now, if-if you can achieve positronic distillation of sub-atomic particles y’know before he does, then he can come back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can give it a shot.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Central Perk, David has a cab waiting to take him to the airport and he’s saying goodbye to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Yay! Look! There’s a piece that doesn’t have floor on it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Stick to your side!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, come on now!

scenes.scene_description: The Hallway, Chandler and Rachel are on their knees with forks trying to salvage what they can of the cheesecake off of the floor.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, what are we havin’?
lines.stage_direction: pulls out a fork Starts digging in.

scenes.scene_description: Joey finishes climbing the stairs and sees them. Chandler and Rachel both stop and look up at him. Joey sits down on the step.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wait I forgot my wrap.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Oh, okay. Wait here.
lines.stage_direction: Goes to get it, but before he gets there Aunt Millie sits down on it forcing him to pull it out from behind her which gets her attention.

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Hi sweetie! Are you leaving?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well…

lines.speaker: Aunt Millie
lines.text: Give us a kiss. Come on! Come on!

scenes.scene_description: Cousin Frannie’s Wedding Reception, Ross and Monica are at the door and about to leave.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?! Why on the lips?!
lines.stage_direction: He wipes his mouth on Monica’s wrap and walks off, leaving Aunt Millie stunned.

scenes.scene_description: Ross hesitates then leans down trying to get her to kiss his cheek, but she moves his head around and kisses him on the lips again.

title: The One Where They’re Up All Night

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, when’s this comet thing start?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, technically it seven billion years ago…
lines.stage_direction: Well, technically you’d be able to see it for days, well nights; that is if you could see it with all of the bright lights of New York.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh no! Oh no!
lines.stage_direction: groaning They all start to get up a leave.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Fine, I’ll stop! No teaching, okay? We’ll just watch the pretty light streaking across the sky. Okay? Who’s official name is Bapstein-King.
lines.stage_direction: Comets don’t streak across the sky, meteors do.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There it is! Oh, look at that! Isn’t Mother Nature amazing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s a plane!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, all right. 1700 bags of peanuts flying that high, that’s pretty amazing too.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey, I wonder if you can see my apartment from up here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No, you can’t.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I don’t-I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man, look at all those stars! Infinite space. It really, really makes you wonder, doesn’t it?
lines.stage_direction: Yeah, you can see what? Five of them from the city?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know what else makes you wonder?
lines.stage_direction: looking through his binoculars at a nearby building

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Check out the rack on this chick!
lines.stage_direction: Turns around to point it out to Ross and finds that Ross is glaring at him. So he quickly puts his binoculars to his eyes and starts looking for the comet.

scenes.scene_description: The Building’s Roof, the entire gang plus Tag are there to look for a comet. They’re looking for a comet on a roof of a New York apartment building. Yeah, that’s realistic. You might as well look for the moon on a bright sunny day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, we’ve been out here for two hours and we haven’t seen any stupid comets. Can we go now? I mean, Chandler’s getting chilly.
lines.stage_direction: She walks over to where Chandler is bundled up in a big coat and shivering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I’m not!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then why are you wearing Monica’s jacket?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because it’s flattering! Come on Monica! Come on Monica!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah actually, I think we’re gonna take off too. We rented a movie.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! I won’t say, ‘no’ to a movie!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh Pheebs, we just actually kinda wanted to be alone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shh! Get me out of here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.
lines.stage_direction: They leave, leaving just Joey and Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come here, check this out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Is it the comet?
lines.stage_direction: Runs over to where Joey’s standing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No-no. Look, there’s a bug stuck in tar right here.
lines.stage_direction: Bends down to get a closer look.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey come—I can’t believe—I bring you here to see the Bapstein-King comet, one of nature’s most spectacular phenomenon, and all you care about are bugs stuck in tar and-and some woman!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know, there’s two women dude.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Show me where?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right-right up here.
lines.stage_direction: Starts looking at them through a piece of pipe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey where’s the pipe that was holding the door open?
lines.stage_direction: noticing the pipe and looking at the door

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know! Yeah, I do.
lines.stage_direction: annoyed Goes back to looking through the pipe. Pause

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! All right—Hey! Don’t look at me! You’re the one who wanted to come up and look for some stupid Burger King comet!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s called the Bapstein-King comet, okay? Hey! Hey! Bapstein was a very well respected astronomer!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no! No! No!
lines.stage_direction: covering his ears and yelling He starts banging on the door.

scenes.scene_description: The Roof, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She’s sleeping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know, just quick-quick question, quick question. Which one was Deep Impact and which one was Armageddon ?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Deep Impact was the one with Robert Duval, Armageddon is what’s going to happen to you if you wake me up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry, I just…can’t sleep. Ooh! Where is that book that you are reading with the two women who were ice-skating and wearing, wearing those hats with the flowers on it? Because every time I look at that cover I’m like…
lines.stage_direction: Turns on the light and Monica groans. Fake snores.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It is in the living room where there is also a light! And no one will kick you in the shin.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! Ow!
lines.stage_direction: Monica kicks him in the shin. He gets out of bed and heads into the living room.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler’s bedroom, Monica is sleeping and Chandler’s sitting in bed, wide awake.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Please don’t be a space ship. Please don’t be a space ship. Oh thank God! How could you be beeping?! I just disconnected you! I took out your battery! How can…
lines.stage_direction: crossing her fingers and closing her eyes She turns on the light and looks around and finds that it’s the smoke detector that’s beeping. She moves a chair over and starts to investigate how to make the beeping turn off, in frustration she yanks the thing off of the wall. She sets it down and heads for bed, just as she gets there it beeps again. She opens the cover and removes the battery, but it still beeps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t interrupt me!!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's apartment, there is a beeping noise coming from the living room and Phoebe sleepily goes to investigate.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rachel!! Monica!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on!

scenes.scene_description: The Roof, Ross and Joey are banging on the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t believe this!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right well, y’know…I guess we know what we have to do to get down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I guess we don’t have a choice. Help us! Please help us! We’re stuck up on the roof and we can’t get down!!!
lines.stage_direction: standing at the edge of the roof Screaming to the street

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross. I was thinking we could just go down the fire escape.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know, I wasn’t finished. But don’t worry! We’re gonna go down the fire escape!!
lines.stage_direction: To Joey Joey motions him to finish. Yelling at the street

scenes.scene_description: Ross gets fed up with Joey’s banging and stops him by pulling him away from the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shhhhhhhhh!!! I’m sorry, I thought maybe I’d make some warm milk and it would help me sleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: With a wok? I thought you were going to read my boring book to put you asleep.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It got interesting! Damn you Oprah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here, let me make the milk, I’m up anyway.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y’know what we can do? Y’know, now that we are up? We can just like talk to each other all night long, y’know like we did when we were first going out. It’d be fun!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay that does sound like fun.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so how bummed were you when the second sister died huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The second sister dies?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. No, I-I was, I was talking about the book I was reading.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The second sister dies in Archie and Jughead Double Digest ?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s correct.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's kitchen, Chandler has a jug of milk in his hands and decides to make some warm milk. He opens up the cabinet to get a pot and manages to knock several other pots onto the floor making a lot of noise.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You wanna go in the bedroom? It’s a little more comfortable.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Oh wait! Umm, did you send those contracts to Milan?
lines.stage_direction: They start to head for the bedroom

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: If this is your idea of sexy talk?
lines.stage_direction: Shakes his head that it’s not working.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No seriously, y’know the contracts I gave you, did you overnight them?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What contracts?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay please tell me that this is just one of your jokes that you do that I don’t get.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Like what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, like the thing when you put the phone in your pants? Tag! I’m serious! This isn’t funny! Those contracts absolutely had to go out today!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Rach, I’m sorry, but you didn’t give me any contracts!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes I did! And I put a little Post-It on it that said, "Must go out today," and underlined today three times and, and then I put a little heart in the corner because I didn’t want to seem to bossy.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I’m telling you, you never gave them to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know what Tag, if we went down to the office you would see those contracts sitting on your desk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No, I would see you looking embarrassed because they are not on my desk!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Or maybe you would see me looking embarrassed because you are talking on the phone with your crotch!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: You wanna go down to the office right now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Come on its late, we’re not gonna go down to the office.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay I understand. I wouldn’t want to be proved wrong either.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay get your coat! Oh! When did you unhook this? Nice work!
lines.stage_direction: They get their coats and start to leave. Rachel suddenly stops and sticks the hand up the back of her shirt. Her bra.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel and Tag are making out on the couch.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, it won’t go down any further. It’s stuck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ugh. Well, we’re just gonna have to jump. Yeah. Now, we’re gonna have to make sure to land to the right of that patch of ice, okay? Not hit the dumpster on the other side and uh, and try to avoid that-that weird brownish red stuff in the middle. So, when you get down there… you go up to the roof and you let me in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh whoa-whoa wait a minute! I have to do it?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Oh yeah, you’ll be fine! It-it’ll be uh, just like bungy jumping. Y’know? But instead of bouncing back up you-you won’t.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What if I smack my head on the concrete?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I’m gonna lie to you Joey, it’s a possibility.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know Ross! I-I tell you what, let’s flip to see who does it, okay? You-you call it in the air, all right?
lines.stage_direction: looks at the ground and at Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, all right. Tails! Can you-can you see what it is?
lines.stage_direction: Joey flips the coin. The coin bounces off of the landing above them and falls to the ground.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Well, you be careful.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? No! No Ross! No-no! Stop! I’m not jumping! Okay, look I have an audition tomorrow and I can’t go if I break my leg.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I’m jumping! I have a son! Okay? He won’t have a father if-if I die!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well all right so, it looks like we’re even!

scenes.scene_description: The Fire Escape, Joey and Ross have reached the last landing. Joey is tugging on the ladder that extends to the ground, but it won’t budge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. So, this wire is connected to this wire which plugs into here. Okay so, to get the beeping to stop all I have to do… Well done, Pheebs. What do you want from me ?!!!!!!!
lines.stage_direction: She points at each as she says it. She picks up a shoe and proceeds to pummel the smoke detector. She then gets up and heads to bed, stops, quickly turns around, and is satisfied that the beeping has stopped. She resumes her trek to bed, but is stopped at the entrance to the hallway by the now steady and extremely loud tone emanating from the smoke detector. Yelling.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's apartment, Phoebe is still investigating the smoke detector trying to figure out how to stop the beeping.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, here’s your milk. What do you want to talk about?
lines.stage_direction: She sees that Chandler has fallen asleep and slams the door loudly to wake him up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? What? What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh! Ohhhh! Were you sleeping sweetie? I’m sorry. Here.
lines.stage_direction: Hands the mug of milk to him.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's bedroom, Monica is entering with a mug.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay! Feel free to look, but I’m telling you those contracts are not on this desk.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh how can you possibly know? Look at this mess, Tag! I mean, this is what I’m talking about! You have to be organized! You’ve got newspapers! You’ve got magazines! You got—Ohh! And who is this chippy? A little young for you Tag, but whatever.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It’s my sister.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, very cute braces. Anyway y’know what, the point is Tag, start looking because you are going to find those contracts on your desk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: So when do you imagine you gave them to me? In the morning or in the afternoon?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: In the afternoon. Mr. Zelner came into my office after lunch. He put them on my desk, and then I put a Post-It on it that said, "Must go out today." So you just keep looking in there! All right?
lines.stage_direction: Looks down onto her desk and finds the folder with the Post-It on it that contains the contracts she imagined she gave Tag

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s Outer Office, Tag and her are arriving.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It’s not here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Puzzler. A bit of a puzzle. Why don’t you um, check the copy room, maybe you left the contracts in there?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: How could I have left them in the copy room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know Tag! How can your genitals make phone calls? Okay? It’s not a perfect world! Just go please.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. Hello? I still don’t get it.
lines.stage_direction: He leaves and she proceeds to plant the folder in his bottom drawer. She then picks up the phone and holds it to her breasts. Hangs up the phone.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s outer office, Tag has finished searching his desk and Rachel comes out to try to plant the folder on the desk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you still awake?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! You?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You do know that was me who just said that right? Hey. As long as we’re both up…
lines.stage_direction: He doesn’t respond and she turns on the light, waking him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah? I hope you’re not thinking about cleaning the living room.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's bedroom, Chandler has his eyes closed, while Monica is fully awake.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, I’m starving! What the hell was I thinking at dinner?! "Do you want soup or salad?" Both! Always order both!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know, y’know I’m lookin’ and I don’t think anyone’s home here. I say we just break the window, crawl through, and-and y’know explain later.
lines.stage_direction: looking in the window behind them

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah? Really? No one’s home?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t think so. Hello? When you get in there…
lines.stage_direction: Knocks on the glass, which angers the big, large, angry dog behind the glass and causes them to jump to the other side of the landing. Joey nods his disapproval.

scenes.scene_description: The fire escape, Joey and Ross are still trying to figure out how to get down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is where you and I part ways. Noisy bitch!
lines.stage_direction: She drops the blanket into the chute.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe’s apartment building, in desperation she has wrapped up the smoke detector in a blanket and is going to throw it into the trash chute.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you know what just happened?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. We-we had sex and then we fell asleep.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. We were in the middle of sex…and you fell asleep.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo! No, that’s not true. No, best time ever! Yeah, you rocked me world!
lines.stage_direction: She turns out the light to go back to sleep.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was giving you some of my best moves, and you missed it. So please wake up so we can do it right!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I’m ready. Come on big fella!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Give me the good stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! No! No! No! Don’t fall asleep! Okay, I am going to make you some coffee. And I probably won’t spill coffee grounds all over the kitchen floor.
lines.stage_direction: Monica falls asleep Monica doesn’t move as he gets out of bed and as he’s heading for the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I’m up! I’m up!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's bedroom, Chandler is turning on the light to awaken a now sleeping Monica.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! I got you some coffee. To, uh… …fair enough. So! Do you got anything for me?
lines.stage_direction: She looks for a place to set it on his messy desk and he clears a spot for her to set it down.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Still no luck.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Did you check your entire desk! Did you check all the drawers!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Do you want me to check again?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, I wish that you would. Well, no it’s not in there! How about that drawer?
lines.stage_direction: He opens the top drawer. Closes it. She points to the bottom one and he opens it. She doesn’t see the folder she planted and bends over to check.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Well, it’s not out here. Is there any chance it could be in your office?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, I don’t-I don’t know. Let me, let me check.
lines.stage_direction: thinks As she heads for her office, she stops glances over her should at Tag, looks into her office, and finds the folder on her desk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can I see you in my office for a minute?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah? You found them!! Y’know what? I’m not even going to gloat. I’m just really relived this whole thing is over.
lines.stage_direction: entering She holds up the folder Rachel is not amused, because she’s still going to try to blame him for her mistake like every ‘good’ boss.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You put these on my desk!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I did not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh really? So you’re saying they just slid out of your bottom drawer, crawled across the floor, then jumped on to my desk?!
lines.stage_direction: I think Dogbert should have a line here.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: How did you know they were in my bottom drawer?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am so hot for you right now.
lines.stage_direction: pause as she realizes her lame attempt to shift the blame has failed

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s outer office, she’s returning with two coffee cups in hand to find Tag sitting there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! How did you get back here?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fire alarm? Oh! Hi, officer—fireman, can-can I help you?
lines.stage_direction: scared She opens the door to reveal a fireman holding the blanket with the smoke detector.

lines.speaker: The Fireman
lines.text: We found your fire alarm in the trash chute.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s not mine.

lines.speaker: The Fireman
lines.text: Yes it is.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How do you know?

lines.speaker: The Fireman
lines.text: The next time you want to dump a fire alarm in a trash chute, don’t wrap it in a blanket that says, "Property of Phoebe Buffay not Monica."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay do you—Okay, do you have a search warrant? Because the last time I checked this was still America!

lines.speaker: The Fireman
lines.text: Please reattach this, it’s against the law to disconnect them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! But please God; tell me how to stop them from going off!

lines.speaker: The Fireman
lines.text: There’s a reset button under the plastic cover.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There’s a reset button?! Ugh, thank you! Thank you! There’s a reset button! My God! Why didn’t I see that! Reset button, reset button, where is there a reset button? Oh here it is! Oh! Ohh, God!
lines.stage_direction: He exits and she goes to shut it off. She takes off the plastic cover and looks for the button. Finds it. Picks it up off of the floor. She presses it hard, but of course it would help if the button was still attached to the detector. In frustration she presses it so hard it causes pain in her thumb.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's apartment, there is someone pounding on the door and Phoebe sleepily walks over and answers it. As she nears the door, the pounding stops and she can hear the smoke detector’s wail.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, do-do you have a good grip?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I’m going to start climb down you now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! Just hurry up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Now-now-now should I climb down your front so we’re face to face or-or should I climb down your back so we’re-we’re butt to face.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think face to face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I would say that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Face to face, yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, here I go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

scenes.scene_description: The fire escape, Joey is now hanging off of the bottom rung of the ladder that won’t move and Ross is watching from above.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my… How much do you weigh Ross?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I prefer not to answer that right now, I’m still carrying a little holiday weight.

scenes.scene_description: Ross steps onto the bottom rung of the ladder and then steps on Joey’s chest.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know, when we talked about face to face, I don’t think we thought it all the way through.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So what do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, just shimmy down me and drop!

scenes.scene_description: Ross continues to climb down. He puts his other foot further down on Joey’s torso, but that doesn’t work very well and he’s forced to wrap his legs around Joey. Which then forces Joey to get a nice and close view of Ross’s crotch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: M-maybe I should hang and you can climb down me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah? Maybe we should talk about that for a little while!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s still looks pretty far!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s not that far! Just drop!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do not rush me!!

scenes.scene_description: Ross continues his trek south, and when they get face to face.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, you should know that my pants are startin’ to come down and I’m not wearing any underwear!

scenes.scene_description: Ross continues south and his now wrapped around Joey’s legs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oww!! My ankle! I really hurt my ankle! I think I twisted it when I—Ooh, a quarter!

scenes.scene_description: Ross panics and falls off, dropping to the ground with a huge crash.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That really was some of your best work.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hm-hmm, I told you! I can’t believe that I’ve only got two hours before I call in sick for work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I have to be up in seven minutes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you’re not gonna believe this, but if you have seven minutes…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you wanna?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! You get the vacuum cleaner and I’ll get the furniture polish!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's bedroom, they’re cuddling.
She runs off leaving a stunned Chandler behind.

title: The One Where Rosita Dies

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y’know I was thinking of moving the couch over here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why would you want to do that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So that there will be a decent place for me to sit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach, there is a decent place to…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And your lap does not count! Okay? Come on help me move this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. No. No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Rosita does not move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry, Rosita? As in…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: As in Rosita does not move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, it’s just a chair! What’s the big deal?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The big deal is that it is the exact equal distance from the bathroom to the kitchen and it’s at the perfect angle so you don’t get any glare coming of off Stevie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Stevie the TV?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Oh what does he know! Come on Rosita, us chichas got to stick together! You bitch!
lines.stage_direction: Joey sets his beer and bag of chips down and heads into his room. She tries pulling on the back of the chair, until the hinge breaks and the back falls off.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is trying to move Joey’s chair and not having much luck at it as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y’know what’s weird? After you guys get married, when you introduce me to people you’re gonna have to say, "This is my brother-in-law Ross." Not, "My friend Ross," "brother-in-law Ross." That’s weird isn’t it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Couldn’t I just say, "This is Ross?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, do whatever you want.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler and Ross are on the couch talking. Phoebe is getting coffee.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Ross! So, I was checking out the uh, real estate section…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, it looks like mom and dad’s house. Oh, it even has a tree with a broken limb out front and the uh, the window in the attic is…Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? What happened to the window in the attic?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t believe mom and dad are selling the house!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t believe they-they didn’t even tell us!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe I still don’t know what happened to the window in the attic!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe sits down between Chandler and Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, hello dad! Monica and I just saw the house in the paper! Yes we’re surprised! Who did you leave a message with?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry!
lines.stage_direction: knocking on the window while outside Runs off.

scenes.scene_description: Ross calls his parents on his cell phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, Joey I am so sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I told you not to move it! Rach, how would you feel if say, I wanted to move you mom, and you said don’t, and I did it anyway and her head fell off?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, come on—Joey, I’ll buy you a new one! All right? We’ll go down to the store right now and we’ll-we’ll get you a new chair.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She’s not even cold yet!
lines.stage_direction: slowly turning and glaring at her

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But don’t you think Rosita would’ve wanted you to move on? I mean y’know, she did always put…your comfort first.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s true.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey is examining the injury to Rosita while Rachel is apologizing to him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay? You ready?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I… I don’t want Stevie to see her like this.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel turns for the door and makes the "Wow!" face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t believe we have to say goodbye to the house we grew up in. Man, some-some stranger’s gonna be living in my room.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, after 15 years of mom and dad keeping it as a shrine to you, it’s time the velvet ropes came down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They kept your room for a while.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please! Dad turned my room into a gym 20 minutes after I moved out! I gotta say, a tanning bed and a stack of Victoria’s Secret catalogues, not a gym!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, you know they love you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: As much as they love you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I was their first born! They thought she was barren! It’s not my fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh, I hate this year!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What’s wrong with this year?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well okay, it’s already February and I’ve only given two massages and they were both the worst tippers in the world!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was me and Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh that’s right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y’know if you want to pick up some extra cash? Some friends of mine made good money doing telemarketing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that’s a great idea. You’re really good on the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah and yeah, and it would probably be better than the last telephone job I had. Y’know, I probably wouldn’t have to say spank as much.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, like you never called!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Ross and Monica are still going on about the house.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: So basically this is very easy. You read from the script and try to sell as much toner as you possibly can.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I can do that! Oh, by the way, I love my office.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: Why don’t we do a trial run.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay. Umm, all right. Hi, this is Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies, can I speak to your supply manager please?
lines.stage_direction: Picks up the phone and starts reading from the script.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: I’m the supply manager.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, okay I would like to talk to you about your toner needs.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: We don’t need any toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay, well I’m sorry to bother you. Bye-bye. Yeah you’re right, this is easy.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: Okay, what was wrong with that call?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh well, all right…um, no offense, but you were kind of rude.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: They’re always going to tell you they don’t need toner, but that’s okay because whatever they say, you can find the answer to it here in this script.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Supervisor
lines.text: So, I think you’re ready to sell toner, do you have any last questions?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. Oh wait yes! I do, I do have one question. What is toner?

scenes.scene_description: The telemarketing office, Phoebe is getting shown to her desk by the supervisor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey! Joe! Full bag. Beer’s still cold. Something terrible must’ve happened here! Oh no-no-no-no-no-no! Stevie, I was never here!
lines.stage_direction: Sees that he’s not here and starts investigating. He picks up the bag of chips. He picks up the beer. He decides it’s not that important; sits down on Rosita, and the back falls off causing him to flip over. Runs over to Stevie. Runs out.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Chandler is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I’m here!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Hi. God, it seems like just yesterday you guys used to come out to watch me work.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad, we-we can’t believe you’re selling the house.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, it’s time for a new family to start their memories here and hopefully their check will clear before they find the crack in the foundation and the asbestos in the ceiling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let’s grab our stuff and get the hell out of here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I’m sorry we can’t store your childhood things anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that’s okay, I can’t wait to see everything again! All of the memories…

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, I don’t know what’s in the boxes down here, but I do know there are six or seven Easy Bake Ovens in the attic.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I used to love to play restaurant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, not as much as you used to love to play uncooked batter eater.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, it is unreasonable to expect a child to wait for a light bulb to cook brownies!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So, I think you’re boxes are over here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Great! Wait, dad who-who’s cigarettes are these?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I don’t know. They-they must be your mother’s, but please, please don’t ask her. I’ll throw these away.
lines.stage_direction: He puts them in his pocket as Ross finds something of interest in one of his boxes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cool! Dad! My report cards! Hey, check this out dad, Math, A. Science, A. History, A. Gym… Oooh, my rock polisher!
lines.stage_direction: reading his grades He puts it away and finds something else.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh look, look there’s your old makeup kit!

lines.text: Well, the white seems to be untouched. Uh-oh.
