lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Y’know how the garage floods every Spring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How are you ever going to sell this place?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: I think I accidentally used Monica’s boxes to keep the water away from the Porsche .

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no. Dad! Dad! What… Oh God…everything’s ruined! Dad, she’s gonna be crushed!
lines.stage_direction: He goes to open one of her boxes and it rips apart.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You don’t secretly smoke do you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: So it’s just your mother then.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, this Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies, can I speak to your supply manager please? Earl, thanks. Hi Earl, this is Phoebe from Empire Office Supplies I’d like to talk to you about your toner needs.
lines.stage_direction: on phone Listens Listens She’s reading from the script.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I don’t need any toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m hearing what you’re saying, but at our prices everyone needs toner.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Not me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: May I ask why?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: You wanna know why. You wanna know why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I surely do!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Okay, I don’t need any toner because I’m going to kill myself.

scenes.scene_description: The telemarketing office, Phoebe is hard at work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, is-is that because you’re out of toner?

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe desperately tries to find the scripted response to that line.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Okay, so…no toner today. Thanks anyway, bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no wait-wait! I can’t just let you hang up! Just please talk to me.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Well…I only have one thing to do today. I guess I could push it back.
lines.stage_direction: He looks at his board in his office that reads, "Today’s Tasks: KILL SELF."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Now, why do you want to kill yourself?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: It’s just that I uh, have been working for ten years now at this meaningless, dead-end job and nobody here even knows I exist!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I-I’m sorry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No look, I-I’m sure that people know you exist!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Oh yeah? I work in a cubicle surrounded by people. I’ve been talking to you for five minutes now about killing myself and no one’s even looked up from their desk. Hang-hang on. Hey everybody! Uh, I’m gonna kill myself! I’ll get back to ya. I got nothing. Wait. Uh, hey Marge! Ehh, nothing. Nothing.
lines.stage_direction: To the people standing around his cubicle. There’s no response; no one even looks up. To Phoebe He sets the phone down. Mimes putting a gun to his head, pulling the trigger, and splattering his brain on the wall behind him. Then points to himself. Marge watches this, then goes back to work. To Phoebe

scenes.scene_description: The telemarketing office, Phoebe is still talking to Earl.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My chair. Now, if anybody asks, your name is Rosita!

lines.text: You will like it!

lines.text: No I won’t.

lines.text: You don’t even know!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because, I know what I like and what I don’t like! It’s not the same thing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well look, if you don’t like this…

lines.text: Come on Joey, I just bought you a new chair! The most expensive one in the store! Hey, y’know what I was thinking? We could name her Francette.

lines.text: Poor thing. Cut down in her prime.

lines.text: Joey, the new chair will be here in an hour. Maybe we should actually move Rosita out of here. Y’know, start the heeling process?

lines.text: Well, I guess you’re right. Maybe, maybe I’ll take her down to the incinerator. It’s gonna be so said, and kinda cool. She’s heeled!

lines.text: That’s weird.

lines.text: No it’s not weird, it’s a miracle!

lines.text: It’s not a miracle Joey! I’m sure there’s some explanation.

lines.text: Joey, I really don’t…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can you tell me how this happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Miracle!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, y’know what? Maybe somebody came in here and fixed it! Or something!

lines.text: Someone like an…angel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That’s right Joey, the chair angel came in and heeled your chair.

scenes.scene_description: They enter their apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Get your non-believer ass outta my chair!
lines.stage_direction: angrily She gets up and heads for her room.

scenes.scene_description: She sits down in the chair.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well, she’ll understand right? It’s not like I did it on purpose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dad that won’t matter to her. Look, all my stuff is safe and dry and all her is-is, is growing new stuff! See, this is exactly the kind of thing that makes her think you guys love me more than you love her.

lines.text: Oh my God, does she really thinks that?

lines.text: Well I don’t know, I-I suppose we may have favored you unconsciously, you were a medical marvel! The doctor said your mother could…

lines.text: Really?

lines.text: Well, not right now. Okay look, Monica came here for some memories and damnit, we’re gonna give her some! Okay, grab…grab some empty boxes. Okay? We’ll-we’ll take stuff from mine and whatever we can pass off as hers we’ll-we’ll put ‘em in their.

lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like uh y’know like this! This! She-she could’ve made this!

lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right? And this! She-she could’ve won this!

lines.text: This could’ve been hers!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Ooh-ooh, what about this?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Your make-up kit? I’d feel better.

lines.stage_direction: grabbing a beer out of the chair’s fridge

It’s got a small refrigerator under one armrest it has phone jacks for the Internet and regular phone, and so much more.

lines.text: All right so Earl, let’s just forget about the people at the office, okay? There-there’s gotta be someone else in your life worth sticking around for! What about-what about your family, your friends, or maybe your girlfriend?

lines.text: No! I’m not finished yet! Don’t! Don’t you dare hang up on me!!!

lines.text: Ohhhhh. That’s how it got fixed!

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.text: What? Wh-hey!

lines.text: Uh-huh! Nice try, but you don’t get that chair anymore! All right? That is my chair now! You can sit on my lap! No I take that back!

lines.text: No-no-no! This chair’s not going anywhere.

lines.text: That’s right!

lines.text: Yeah! We are!

lines.text: Excuse me! Can you tell me where I can find Earl? He’s the supply manager around here.

lines.text: I’m right here!!!!

lines.text: Earl! I’m Phoebe.

lines.text: Umm, look it, you-you can’t kill yourself.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Look, um I really appreciate your coming down…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no I can’t! I can’t let you do it!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because it was fate that made me call you today!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: I thought it was toner.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Think about it okay? This isn’t even my regular job! Okay? And my first day on the job, you’re my first call! And-and somebody else might’ve hung up on you, but I wouldn’t do that because I know about this stuff. My mom killed herself.

lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m not gonna give you tips! Look don’t you see that this-this…this all came together so that I could stop you from doing this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it’s fate!

lines.text: It doesn’t really seem like enough to be fate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Well umm, okay here’s a weird thing. My mother was also a supply manager.

lines.text: I’m actually the office manager.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! So was she! And! Get this, okay? Your-your name is Earl, right? Her name was Pearl, P-Earl.

lines.text: Sure! Umm, where are you from?

lines.speaker: Earl

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! So was she! Oh, I’ve got-I’ve got goose bumps.

lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, y’know I’m wearing layers and it’s warm.

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Yeah-yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But if—no look, okay. These jerks might not care about you, but the universe does! And that says a lot!

lines.speaker: Earl
lines.text: Did you hear that?! I don’t need you guys to care about me! Because the universe cares! The whole universe! I really wished they’d care just a little bit though.

lines.text: Y’know, I don’t-I don’t think it’s you. This is a freaky place. Hey! Guys! Oh no, it’s you.

lines.text: Yeah.

title: The One Where They All Turn Thirty

lines.text: Happy birthday!!!

lines.text: Happy birthday!!!

lines.text: Oh really. Is that how you felt when you turned thirty?

lines.text: Late thirties? Oh come on you guys! Is it just me? Am I overreacting to this?

lines.text: No Rach, it’s not just you. My thirtieth birthday certainly wasn’t that much fun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And now Chandler! We’re all gettin’ so old! Why are you doing this to us?!
lines.stage_direction: screaming Looking up Turns away crying.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rach, you’re in a great place in your life. Come on, you’ve got a great job! Good friends…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you’re roommate is a soap opera star.

lines.text: Look, y’know I know my life’s going pretty well, but I look around and I just see so many people who’ve accomplished so many other goals by the time they’re thirty.

lines.text: Yeah, but you shouldn’t compare yourself to me.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Come on Phoebe! You can do it Phoebe! Come on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There you go!

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, breakfast is finished but Rachel’s still down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I did it! One mile on a hippity-hop! That’s it!! That’s everything I wanted to do before I was thirty. Oh, except I wanted to patch things up with my sister. But oh well. Yay!! And-and girls this thing is a Godsend if you know what I mean.
lines.stage_direction: They all cheer again. Rachel and Monica look intrigued and as they all head into Central Perk, Rachel picks up the hippity-hop that Phoebe left behind.

scenes.scene_description: She crosses the line and they all cheer again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thirty. Ugh, I mean thirty! Monica, do you remember mean, old Mrs. Kreeger in the fifth grade? She was thirty!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing. I don’t want to do anything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, doing nothing on your thirtieth is better than doing something stupid, like Ross.

lines.text: Hey! That was a practical purchase! I needed that car for transportation! Okay? I-I have a child!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How hot do I look in this, huh?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, a sports car? Wouldn’t it have been cheaper to just stuff a sock down there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s not what this is about okay? I-I am a sports car enthusiast. I have always been into cars.

lines.text: Hey, what’s the horsepower on this thing?

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can’t believe you bought this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really! God Ross, what were you thinking? I know it’s really shallow, but a part of me wants him again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well get in line missy. So, can I have a ride stud?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hop in. Get ready for the smoothest ride of your life.

lines.text: Damnit!

lines.text: Okay, who’s next?

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know what? I am going to do something today. I’m not just gonna sit around like some old lady. I’m gonna get something pierced. Like my uh, like my nose or my tongue or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! ‘Cause y’know that hurts.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So what?! Y’know what? The way I see it——Ow! Son of a bitch!!

lines.text: Look Rachel, I know what you’re going through. I’m totally freaked about turning 25.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Get out, get out of my apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right Rach, for what it’s worth, I think that you’re doing great. I mean y’know let’s face it, no one handles this well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Least of all you.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Why? What you’d do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Weren’t you asked to leave sonny?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Would you put that back on?! Monica’s gonna be here any minute!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But it hurt’s my Joey’s Apple.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, for the last time. It’s not named for each individual man.

lines.text: You’ve done a wonderful job with this party Chandler. Everything looks so lovely.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh!

lines.text: Hey! Everybody hide! Hide! I saw her! She’s coming!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Everybody down! Everybody down!
lines.stage_direction: Rachel turns off the lights and everyone crouches. As everyone crouches, a ripping noise erupts from the assemblage.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Crap.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, everybody stay here. I will find out what’s going on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Heyyy!! You got the door open!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey-hey are you drunk?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo! Okay. Whoa! Okay. See I was, I was a little nervous about turning thirty. So the bus boys took me out for some drinks. I wanna puke on you later!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here is the thing. We have thrown a very formal surprise party for you in there! All of your friends are in there and your parents!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Noo!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.text: Oh no! My parents have never seen me drunk! That they know of.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, here’s the thing. We’re gonna get you some coffee and they will never know that you’re drunk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?! You promise?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I’ll take care of it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I love you so much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay we have to do something about your breath.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about your breath?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s still yours. Okay, now remember it’s a surprise party. So, when you go in, act surprised.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I can do that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: He goes out into the hall and finds a very drunk Monica lying up against Joey and Rachel’s door.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Surprise!!!

Monica screams and they all stare at her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, forward. Forward—Stop! Okay, back—Stop! Okay, forward—Stop! Stop! Stop!
lines.stage_direction: The car moves an inch and Ross runs to the back of the car. The car barely moves and Ross runs back to the front.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, just forget about it. This guy’s got you totally wedged in.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Is this yours?

lines.text: Well actually…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no-no! It’s mine! It’s-it’s mine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you soooo need this car.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Okay, here’s what we’re gonna do. Okay, I’m gonna break into this mini-van and put it in neutral. You guys push it forward so Ross can drive out of his spot. Okay? All right, here we go. Haul ass!!!!
lines.stage_direction: running up She opens her coat and reveals that thing car thieves use to break into cars as Ross jumps in behind the wheel. She inserts the device, unlocks the door, opens it, and the alarm goes off.

Runs off.
The rest of the gang runs away, except for Ross who’s tramped inside his car. To hide he puts the top up as Monica, Rachel, and Joey come running past.

lines.text: Happy thirtieth birthday! Here! It’s for the child in you, and the woman. Happy thirtieth!

lines.text: Because it’s our thirtieth birthday.

lines.text: Yeah, no we’re not thirty. We’re 31. Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait!

lines.text: Oh, it’s you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. What?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, we’re not thirty, we’re 31.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nu-uh!

lines.text: Yea-huh! That’s what is says on my birth certificate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You have your birth certificate?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, I got a big box of family stuff when my mom died.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Our mom.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right! Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Do you have my birth certificate?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: No, I sold it to a Swedish runaway.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh my God, we are 31.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just lost a whole year of my life.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Your middle name is Pamela?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I never knew mine. Do you remember what it is?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yes! Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s my first name.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right, okay, then no.

lines.text: Paul!

lines.speaker: Chandler

lines.text: Now, there is a dress laid out on your bed. Okay, you’re doing great. You’re doing great. You’re doing fine.

lines.text: Hey, what’s going on?

lines.text: Monica’s a little drunk.

lines.text: What’s-what’s going on? Phil’s really pissed!

lines.text: Okay, will you just go help her change please!

lines.text: Okay, but taking care of a drunk, naked woman seems like a job for Joey.

lines.text: Yep.

lines.text: Ahh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A scooter!

lines.text: No! No-no, I love it. Thank you.

lines.text: Okay, open ours next. Open ours next!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.text: For my last birthday you gave me a hug ! Okay, read the card! Read the card!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Happy birthday Grandma! It’s better to be over the hill then buried under it. All our love Monica and Chandler. That’s funny, yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no! That was the joke!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I know! I get it! It’s funny!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, because you’re not a grandmother!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No I know, because to be a grandmother you have to be married and have children and I don’t have any of those things. That’s why it’s so funny.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All you had to do was buy the card!

scenes.scene_description: Runs into her room crying.

lines.text: Hey! Look who’s back! It’s the birthday girl! How’s the birthday girl feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I feel fine, but I think you’re bumming out the rest of the kids.

lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! Y’know what? I realized it was stupid to get upset about not having a husband and kids. All I really needed was a plan. See I wanna have three kids…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh let me guess, and you wanna have them all at the same time and you wanna have them for your brother.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: As I was saying… I should probably have the first of the three kids by the time I’m 35 which gives me five years. I love this plan! I wanna marry this plan!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you could do that, I’d marry the hippity-hop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, if I wanna have my kid when I’m 35, I don’t have to get pregnant until I’m 34. Which gives Prada four years to start making maternity clothes! Oh wait, but I do want to be married for a year before I get pregnant…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really! That long?! Look all you want, it’s happening!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler slowly turns and looks at her. To Chandler

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, so I don’t have to get married until I’m 33! That’s three years, that’s three whole years—Oh, wait a minute though. I’ll need a year and a half to plan the wedding, and I’d like to know the guy for a year, year and a half before we get engaged… Which means I need to meet the guy by the time I’m thirty.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Which is fine! Because you just turned——twenty-eight!
lines.stage_direction: Removes two candles from the cake

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Ross, no! It is not fine! Eh-eh-according to my plan I should already be with the guy I wanna marry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you quit hoggin’ it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m telling you it’s like watching Bambi learn how to walk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’re drunk! Mom and dad are gonna be maaaaadd! Maybe I’m a little drunk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that’s great! Right there! Can we get some of that over here please? There we go.

lines.text: How are you feeling?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You are so handsome! I wanna make love to you right here, right now!

lines.text: I really wish that you wouldn’t.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now all you have to do is just get through a little bit more, okay? Then we can put you in bed, okay? Just smile and don’t talk to any one.

lines.text: Speech! Speech! Let’s hear from the birthday girl! Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pheebs!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don’t you see? Everyone’s looking at me! The plan’s working! I didn’t even have to take off my top yet!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Speech! Come on Monica!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Come on! Speech!

lines.text: Hey Chandler, you can’t keep your hands off her for one second!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh-ho, I think it’s nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I think it’s necessary.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I-I-I wanna thank you all for coming. My family and my friends…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wooo!! Hoo!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I really like to say that I’m-um… Y’know what I’d really like to say? I’m drunk!! That’s right mom and dad your little Harmonica is hammered!! And guess what! I’ve been drunk before! And I’ve smoked a cigarette! And I got a box of Ding-Dongs hidden in my underwear drawer! It’s all okay. It’s okay because I turned thirty today. And, and I can do anything I want! Because I am a grown up.
lines.stage_direction: Pause Mrs. Geller pulls the camera down. Ross grabs the camera out of his dad’s hands. Falls over sideways with Ross filming the fall.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay quick, help me get this off!
lines.stage_direction: To Joey Motions to her top.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!!

Ross pans the camera over to Phoebe.

lines.text: I lost a whole year! I can’t believe it! This is so unfair!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I don’t know Pheebs. It’ll be okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Will it? Will it?! I mean, how would you feel if you found out you were 31?

lines.text: That’s not gonna happen. No. Because we have a new deal!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Like okay I-I-I, I haven’t met any Portuguese people! I, I haven’t had the perfect kiss! And I haven’t been to sniper’s school!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, y’know why don’t we just go upstairs and have some birthday cake?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I just feel like being by myself for a while. All right? I’ll see you guys later. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. Oh, poor Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, y’know what you guys? I think I’m gonna go walk her home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh man!

lines.text: What?

lines.text: He’s gonna eat the cake!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs! Wait up! Listen uh, close your eyes. Maybe that’s one thing you can cross off your list.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.text: Oh!

lines.text: Okay, is everybody clear? We’re gonna pick it up…and move it. Now all we need is teamwork, okay? We’re gonna lift the car…and slide it out. Lift and slide!

lines.text: Ross, I really don’t think…

lines.text: Okay, here we go.

scenes.scene_description: Ross’s birthday, his car is still trapped in it’s spot. Now Joey, Phoebe, and Ross are at the front of the car with Monica, Rachel, and Chandler at the rear of the car.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, can I…

lines.text: Oh, come on Rach! My turn just started!

lines.text: Oh. Okay. Hey, can I ride this outside?

lines.text: Whatever! Okay, I’m not your mother.

lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.text: How are you doing? Are you feeling any better?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I’m doing okay. I’m um…let’s talk.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm…

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What’s up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh Tag, umm…you’re such a great guy and we have sooo much fun together but I don’t-I don’t…

lines.text: Wait! I think I see where you’re going, but before you say anything else, can I just say one more thing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well said. And a uh good example of the fun I was referring to uhh, but I just think I’m past the point where I think I can y’know, just have fun.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Rachel, don’t do this. This is just because you’re turning thirty.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it is! But you’re just a kid! I mean you’re 25!

lines.text: Twenty-four actually.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God! Y’know what I wish? I wish you were six years older. Well actually, if I’m wishin’ for stuff, I actually wish I was six years younger.

lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! How’d it go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, if I only want two kids, can I keep him for another year?

title: The One With Joey’s New Brain

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m glad you’re here, we have a couple of things to ask you about the wedding ceremony.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What’s the matter? You okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it’s just y’know…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica said wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, so anyway, we thought one of you could read something during the ceremony.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! I would love to read a poem.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you think you could get through a poem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’ll be a short one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so Ross will be doing the reading.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohhh…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Yeah, I guess, I guess I could do that too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I kind of uh, have something else planned for you guys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you mind telling us what it is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry, I’m kinda keeping this one on the Q.T.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, whatever it is, I hope it involves winking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So I just talked to one of the DOOL writers today, and…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is DOOL?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Days Of Our Lives . Anyway, you’re not gonna believe it! My character is coming out of his coma!!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And-and-and not only that, I’m gettin’ a new brain!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So great things are happening at work and in your personal life!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, what do you mean you’re getting a new brain?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, they’re killing off one of the characters on the show, and when she dies her brain is being transplanted into my body.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? A brain transplant?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, it’s a highly controversial procedure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s ridiculous!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I think it’s ridiculous that you haven’t had sex in three and a half months.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s winter, they are fewer people on the street.
lines.stage_direction: to Monica and Rachel Rachel and Monica smile and nod, knowingly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who are they killing off?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh Cecilia Monroe, she plays Jessica Lockhart.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She’s my favorite character on DOOL.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She is so good at throwing drinks in people’s faces, I mean I don’t think I’ve ever seen her finish a beverage.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the way she slaps all the time!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wouldn’t you love to do it just once?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don’t do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cecilia Monroe man, what a great actress.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, tell me about it. And she’s been on the show forever, it’s gonna be really hard to fill her shoes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, help me out here, when you come out of the "brain transplant," you are going to be her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, but in Drake Remoray’s body. Why is this so hard for you to get? I thought you were a scientist!

lines.text: Rach, so, that guy there. Straight or gay?

lines.text: Yeah? Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well mainly because he's kissing that other guy.

lines.text: Oh no not that guy there. That guy right there.

lines.text: Oh yeah he's too cute to be straight.

lines.text: Ohh knockers will help us figure it out.

lines.text: What if, um, if he calls his own cell phone to find out who found it and I answer and we start talking and we fell in love. I mean wouldn't that be a great story? Kind of like a fairy tale for the digital age.

lines.text: Yeah… That does sound great. I'm going to get the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.text: I don't know. I haven't been out on a date in so long.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Okay, see? I get the phone.

lines.text: No way! No way! You just broke with Tag a week ago.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nice try.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hey-hey wait! How do we fairly decide who gets the phone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t know.

lines.text: Well umm, maybe we could uhh… Ah-ha! Too slow!!

lines.text: Can I get some ice here?!

lines.text: Oh anyway, I just wanted to say how wonderful I think you are.

lines.text: You’re not the fan who’s dying are you?

lines.text: I’m supposed to meet and hug a fan whose dying, but that’s not supposed to be until later!

lines.text: No. No, I’m Joey Tribbiani; we did a scene together yesterday. I-I’m the guy in the coma!

lines.text: Oh that was a real person?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: An-an-anyway I-I just wanted to say that since I’m getting your brain when you leave the show, I was wondering if there was any tips you can give me…

lines.text: I-I-I’m leaving the show?

lines.text: I don’t know. Why? Did you hear something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.text: Ms. Monroe… Oh there you go.

lines.text: No Phoebe! You cannot get the phone that way; that’s not fair! Okay look, I have an idea. Why don’t we, why don’t we see what kind of number he has on his speed dial, and then from that we can tell who has more in common with him. And then whoever does gets the phone.

lines.text: Oh you’re right. Thank you! What’s your name again?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joey.

lines.text: Joey, well thank you. That is so sweet. Oh, excuse me.

lines.text: Uh yeah-ye-ye-ye-ye-ye—l-l-l-l-l-look the-the-the only reason that I, that I came up to you before was because well, I’m really nervous about-about being you. Y’know if you can help me capture the essence of the character. Y’know? Help me keep Jessica alive. Please?

lines.text: All right Joey, I will help you. Not because I-I owe it to this stupid show , but because I owe it to Jessica.

lines.text: No! No-no they’re not. They’re still very angry! But y’know Chandler is also half-Swedish. You know what the Swedish people are famous for? Sitting down and being quiet.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: So, the essence of the character is rooted in her confidence. So, when Jessica enters a room for instance, she owns everything and every person in that room. You try.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! All right!
lines.stage_direction: He scurries out the set door and re-enters, extremely impressed

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: No, he already knows that he owns everything in the room! He’s not finding it out for the first time! So, try it again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay.
lines.stage_direction: He goes out and comes back in, glaring at everything.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Right. He’s not angry at the room either. Try it again, he owns it! He owns the room. It is his. He owns, owns, owns, owns the room! He owns it!! All right, it’s a little weird, but it’s getting better. Oh well, I’m gonna miss this woman so much. I don’t know what I’m going to do! I mean, it’s been 20 years of my life.
lines.stage_direction: Joey gets a snooty look on his face. Joey is pleased.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well—Hey-hey! Maybe, maybe uh, maybe this is a good thing. Y’know? It’ll-it’ll give you a chance to shake things up, play different characters. You’re so talented.

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I am. I am, but I don’t know you know. An actor of a certain age is not that easy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey that’s not true! Look at uh, look at Angela Lansb—Angelina Jolie!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: I probably should’ve just left years ago when the offers were pouring in, but y’know I just got so comfy here! And… Ohh, I turned down some amazing work!

lines.text: Like-like what?

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: Well, let’s just say if I left 15 years ago, the landscape of Mexican cinema would be very different today!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Cecilia
lines.text: But… Well now, now’s a different time for me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh hey come on, don’t-don’t-don’t do this! Umm, look let-let me tell you something, okay? Now when I watch you do a scene, I’m thinking, "Boy, she-she is a great actress!" Uh but-but, I am also thinking, "She is hot!"

lines.text: You own the room. We should probably get-get uh…

lines.text: Yeah! Okay.

lines.text: That was good, that was really good. But I-I think your hands maybe a little off, they should be maybe right like…

lines.text: Okay! Umm, I uh, I’m your teacher. I’m sorry, you’re-you’re a student and I-and I like women. In spite of what may be written on the backs of some of these chairs.

lines.text: And the winner is…Joey Tribbiani! Oh… Wow! I honestly never expected this. I uh, I didn’t prepare a speech. But umm, I’d like to thank my parents, who’ve always been there for me. I’d also like to thank my friends, Chandler, Monica, Phoebe, Rachel…

Hey Phoebe and I were just talking about how our relationship is deep and meaningful. It really is don’t you think?

lines.text: John Wheeler from General Hospital , Gavin Grant from The Young and the Restless , Dunkin Harrington from Passions , and Joey Tribbiani from Days of Our Lives . And the Soapie goes to……Gavin Grant from The Young and the Restless !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Any one of the brilliant actresses nominated for this award tonight deserves to take it home. Unfortunately only one can. The nominees for Best Supporting Actress are from Passions Erin Goff. From One Life to Live Mary Loren Bishop , from All My Children Sarah Mchann, and from Days of Our Lives Jessica Ashley. And the winner is……Jessica Ashley from Days of Our Lives . Uh, unfortunately Jessica couldn’t be with us tonight so I’ll be accepting this award on her behalf. And I’m sure that Jessica would like to thank my parents who always believed in me. She’d also like to thank my friends, Chandler, Monica, Ross, Phoebe, and Rachel who’s sittin’ right there!
lines.stage_direction: angrily and monotone Shakes his head in disgust. There is applause, which Joey disgustedly waits to dye out. They start to applaud again, but Joey interrupts in with the rest of the nominees Opens the envelope Applause Realizes something Points at Rachel. The music starts and his microphone is turned off, this angers Joey again and he disgustedly exits.

lines.text: All right, I’ve never been engaged and I’ve never really been married, but I can only tell you what my mother told me. Whenever you have doubts or fears or anxieties about a relationship, do not communicate them to your husband.

lines.text: Okay, but all right you’re a guy, does it not freak you that you’re never gonna sleep with anybody else?

Don’t make this worse and I’ll give you a C. Shall we?

lines.text: I’m a party planner. I’ll see you tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay!
lines.stage_direction: Joins the rest of the gang.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey guys! Look who’s back! It’s Ray-ray!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shut up that was my friend Melissa from college.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She seems really , really fun!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: She’s actually very sweet and we used to be very close.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute, she isn’t… She’s not the one who you…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who you what? Who you what?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow? Wow what?! Wow what?! Who you—what?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s not a big deal!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They were lovers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No we weren’t! It was nothing! It was one night, senior year we went to a party, had a lot of sangria and y’know, ended up…kissing for a bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So that’s two of my wives.

scenes.scene_description: A woman walks up to Gunther in the background.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay so this is where the band is. And this is where the bar is. And all these pins have people’s names on them. And Rach, here you are.
lines.stage_direction: telling Chandler the seating arrangement Points. Points She has pins to show the seating at each of the tables. Points to Rachel’s place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow. Why don’t we just take me and put me with a Manhattan in my hand, talking to the cute bartender. These pins aren’t for playing are they?
lines.stage_direction: Grabs her pin Puts her pin at the bar and laughs. Monica just glares at her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, the red ones are my guests and the blue ones are yours.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is so sad. I mean, I only have like ten pins.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, relax it’s not a contest. Certainly not a close one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Stop picturing it!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I think that’s it. The seating chart is done. This is our wedding. They all look like they’re having fun don’t they?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, so where are my parents gonna be?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Let’s see, well…if this is the wedding hall then umm you’re parents will be at home in Queens.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What they’re not invited?! Oh no, that’s terrible! They’re gonna be crushed!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why would they think they’re invited?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got me. I don’t…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I’m sorry. I thought parents were coming! Y’know? Your parents are comin’! Chandler’s parents are comin’! Ross’s parents are comin’!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross’s parents are my parents!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well-well—see? Parents are comin’!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know I think we should invite them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh please, you just want more blue pins.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well this is just sad!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, all right. Maybe I can fit them in if I just do some rearranging. But uh, Rachel may actually have to sit at the bar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is not a problem.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe you’ll order a little sangria?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, get out of here!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is working on the seating chart while Chandler looks on and Rachel reads.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So now, these are all the tuxedos that we make and if there’s anything that you like, we can make you a deal. Anything at all. But these are the three that Monica pre-approved.
lines.stage_direction: motioning to a rack Grabs a few

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, thanks a lot for hookin’ me up Rach. I want you to know that I want you to attend our wedding as my guest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m Monica’s maid of honor. Okay? Don’t try to blue pin me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what’s the deal with these? These-these look nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh they are nice. We-we custom-make tuxedos for celebrities and then when they’re done with them they just send ‘em back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean like for award shows?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Some of them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean these tuxes have been down the red carpet with people yelling, "You are you wearing?! You look fabulous!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, might I suggest watching a little more ESPN and a little less E! ?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, who wore those?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, well let’s see uh, this one is Tom Brokaw.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This one is uh Paul O’Neil.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who’s that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He plays for the Yankees. Seriously, ESPN ! Just once and a while, have it on in the background. Ooh, this one was Pierce Brosnan!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler nods and Rachel grabs another tux

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pierce Brosnan?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 007?! This is James Bond’s tux?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I have to get married in James Bond’s tux!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s a pretty cool tux.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it’s not just that, I would be England’s most powerful weapon. Jet setting heartbreaker on her majesty’s secret service. A man who fears no one; with a license to kill. Would Monica let me wear this?

scenes.scene_description: Ralph Lauren , Rachel is showing Chandler the selection of tuxedos.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We should really learn how to play the real way.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I like our way. Oh! Chess!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs a piece and jumps a bunch of Joey’s like in Checkers.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nice move.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So Joey I just hooked Ross and Chandler up with some tuxedos for the wedding, do you need one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I’m performing the ceremony. I’m not wearing a tux.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what are you going to wear?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Multi-colored robes! Ooh, and maybe a hat.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Does Monica know about this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t think so.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can I please be there when you tell her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey oh, Rach wait! Do you want to go to a movie tonight?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, y’know what? I can’t. I have to have dinner with that Melissa girl.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I come?! I won’t even talk! You’ll just hear the noise from my video camera.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is this? What’s going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh good! Can I tell her?! Can I tell her?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, do you want to hear what actually happened or Joey’s lewd version?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey’s!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, come on! I had this friend from college and I made the stupid mistake of telling Joey that one time…she and I y’know…kissed a little bit.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I’m sure that happened.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It-it did!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. It happened!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, it was senior year in college. It was after the Sigma Chi luau and Melissa and I got very drunk! And we ended up kissing! For several minutes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which means she had a couple spritzers and a quick peck on the cheek.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why are you taking this away from me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, why is it so hard for you to believe?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! I just—I didn’t know that you are a lesbian.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not saying that I’m a lesbian! I’m just saying that this happened!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, it just seems pretty wild and you’re so—y’know so…vanilla.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Vanilla?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m not vanilla! I’ve done lots of crazy things! I mean I got-I got drunk and married in Vegas!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: To Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, y’know what? If you don’t want to believe me about this, why don’t you just come with me to dinner tonight and she will tell you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! All right! Yeah! ‘Cause I just can’t picture it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-ho, you should get inside my head.

scenes.scene_description: Joey and Rachel's, Joey and Phoebe are moving chess pieces around on the board and hitting the timer at random.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Guess what I got for your wedding!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: A freakish thin date with a hanger for her head?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. Rachel hooked me up with a tux! But not just any tux, Batman’s tux!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s right! Made expressly for Val Kilmer and worn by him in the hit film…that Batman film he was in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can’t wear that! I’m wearing the famous tux! James Bond’s tux!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So—If you wear that you’ll make mine less special.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you need something to make this day special? Hello! You-you-you have the most special thing of all! You are marrying the woman you love.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler is on the couch reading as Ross enters carrying a garment bag.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please, don’t take away my cool thing. Please?! Pretty please?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pretty please? Not very uh, 007.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, it’s my wedding day okay? If you were getting married I would never do anything to upset you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: When I got married you slept with my sister.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was pretty 007.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler mimics him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey. Oh good-good you’re here! All right, I figured it out. I’m gonna take two tables of eight, I’m gonna add your parents, and I’m gonna turn them into three tables of six. Okay? And I called the caterer; I added two extra meals, we are good to go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, they’re not coming.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Somehow they got the idea that you only invited them because of me. They…feel a little unwanted.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that’s too bad. It’s true, but too bad.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look Mon, if you could just call my mom…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! Look just-just tell—let her know that you really want them to be there. Let’s not forget, this is a woman that has sent you many lasagnas over the years.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No she hasn’t.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it her fault that some of them didn’t make it to you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what am I going to say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know. Just uh, just tell ‘em it was a mix-up with the invitations, or—No-no-no! Blame it on the post office. They hate the post office. And the Irish! But I don’t think you can blame it on them so…
lines.stage_direction: He dials the phone and hands it to Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? Yeah, hi! Mrs. Tribbiani? Hi, this is Monica Geller. Yeah I’m just calling to say that Chandler and I uh, really hope you can make it to the wedding. Yeah, apparently a bunch of invitations that we sent weren’t delivered. Umm, I guess there was some screw up at the damn post office! T-Tell me about it! Yeah, yeah, the US Post Office? No, more like US lost office! What are they Irish?!
lines.stage_direction: on phone Listens Listens Listens Joey nods his approval. Listens Listens Listens Joey gives her a thumbs up.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is still working on the seating chart as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: …anyway, his name is Allan and we’ve been going out for three years. He was my first client when I became a party planner. He was planning a party for his girlfriend at the time. Oh well. And he was Theta Beta Pi at Syracuse.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh, that’s great!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Hmm Phoebe, were you ever in a sorority?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course! Yeah, I was uh, umm Thigh Mega Tampon.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: What one?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Y’know, we were really huge too, but then they had to shut us down when Regina Philange died of alcohol poisoning.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Oh, isn’t a shame when one girl ruins it for the whole bunch?

scenes.scene_description: A restaurant, Melissa, Rachel, and Phoebe are talking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Anyway, speaking of drinking too much. I was uh, tellin’ Phoebe about that one crazy night after the Sigma Chi luau where you and I uh, we made out.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Remember?! We—come on both had the sarongs on, and we had the-the coconut bikini tops…

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …we went back the house and we got really silly and we…we made out.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Oh wow, Ray-ray I have no idea what you’re talking about.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe agrees by grunting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross is Batman!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, he did manage to keep his identity secret for a long time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Rachel got Ross the tuxedo that Val Kilmer wore in Batman. Okay Batman is so much cooler than James Bond!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?! 007 has all those gadgets!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Batman has a utility belt!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 007 has a fancy car!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Batman has the Batmobile!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 007 gets all the ladies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Batman has Robin! We get ESPN right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about you go put on your 007 tuxedo and I’ll make you a nice martini.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Actually, I don’t like martinis.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How about a Youhoo with a funny straw?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, yum!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Listen, I know the invitation says 6
lines.text: 00, but does that mean that you want people to get there at six, or the show is gonna start right at six?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The show?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right. Right. The wedding, gotcha. But I mean, it’s gonna start a little late right? I mean, weddings start late. Right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have you ever been to one of my weddings?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah. Yeah. Well look, the thing is it’s the same day as my niece’s christening and I really want my parents to be there in time to see me. ‘Cause my part’s just in the beginning I’m not even in the rest of the show—Wedding!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The wedding starts at six.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. Okay, I totally hear ya. Oo how about this? I vamp a little ‘til they get there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’ll vamp?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah y’know, like warm up the crowd. Ask ‘em where they’re from. ‘Cause in Joey Tribbiani you get a minister and you get an entertainer. I’m a minis-tainer! There is no one better! There is no one greater!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is on the couch as Chandler disgustedly enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How can you not remember us kissing?!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: I don’t know. I don’t remember a lot of things that never happened.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh… Come on! Remember? We were on the sleeping porch! We couldn’t stop giggling? And our coconuts kept knockin’ together?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, somewhere Joey’s head is exploding.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah—but come on—Listen, I’m sorry I don’t want to make you uncomfortable, but I told Phoebe that it happened and she doesn’t believe me.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: I’m sorry Ray-ray. I mean if I thought it happened I would say it. Maybe I passed out and you did stuff to me while I was sleeping.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel, it’s okay. You don’t have to do this. I believe you. All right? Okay, if-if you say that you kissed Melissa, then you kissed Melissa.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: She didn’t.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know.

scenes.scene_description: The restaurant, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You just carry that around?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes. I find it to be something of a conversation piece.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Between you and…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gunther. Hey-hey! Why don’t we put them on? Y’know get a picture of Batman and James Bond together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I would but mine doesn’t fit. The pants are a little tight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A little tight? I could see double-oh and seven in those pants.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well that stinks. I was looking forward to us wearing our celebrity tuxes together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, does that mean that you’re not going to wearing yours?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you kidding? It’s Batman’s tux!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me try it on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, but just the jacket. Double-oh and seven are not gettin’ in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Holy double-vented comfort Batman! What’s this?
lines.stage_direction: trying on the jacket Finds something in the pocket

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: An invitation for the At First Sight premiere? Oh my God! Val Kilmer didn’t wear this in Batman! He wore it to the premiere of some tooty-fruity love story where he played a blind guy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let me see that! Oh man!
lines.stage_direction: Grabs the invitation and reads it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The only superpower you have is a slightly heightened sense of smell.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are working on the seating chart as Ross enters carrying his tux around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Uh, Monica? Chandler? Can I talk to you guys for a second?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right that’s it, I give up! Whatever you want you can have it! You wanna sing a song? You wanna do a dance? You want your mom stand at the Alter and scream racial slurs? I don’t care!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, I-I just wanna thank you guys for what you did for my parents, that was really sweet. They’re so happy they get to be a part of your special day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you’re welcome. And tell them we’re really glad they’re coming.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. I will. Ohh! Check out what they got me to wear for the ceremony! Huh? I wear it like this when I marry you guys, and then this is for party time.
lines.stage_direction: Runs to his apartment and returns wearing a rather silly hat. He tilts it to the side of his head

scenes.scene_description: Hands him the jacket and walks away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It happened! I am telling you it happened!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Okay. Well, it was great meeting you. And uh Rachel, I-I don’t think I’ll be calling you because umm, y’know you’ve gotten weird. Take care you guys.
lines.stage_direction: To Phoebe hails a cab The cab pulls up.

scenes.scene_description: Outside the restaurant, Melissa, Phoebe, and Rachel are emerging.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Wait a minute! No wait a minute! Okay? Look, that night was the one wild thing I have ever done in my entire life, and I’m not gonna let you take that away from me! Okay, so if you don’t remember that, maybe you will remember this!
lines.stage_direction: She does so. She grabs Melissa and kisses her on the lips.

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: My God! You love me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Of course I remember our kiss. I think about it all the time. I can still hear the coconuts knockin’ together I… I just didn’t want to tell you ‘cause I didn’t think that you’d return my love, and now that you have…
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe is shocked. Leans in to kiss Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa! Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! Whoa! Whoa!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Aww, look who’s being suddenly shy. You can’t tell me you don’t feel what I feel. Nobody can kiss that good and not mean it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I-I-I’m just…I’m just a good kisser!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry!

lines.speaker: Melissa
lines.text: Oh you don’t have to be sorry. I’m…I’m obviously kidding. I’m not in love with you. I’m not in love with her. I don’t hear coconuts banging together. Yeah, I don’t…picture your face when I make love to my boyfriend. Anyway, I gotta go. Eh…kiss good-bye? No? Okay.
lines.stage_direction: laughs Laughs again To Phoebe Rachel stares at her stunned. Hurries into the cab and drives off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! I mean I had no idea that that was gonna…

scenes.scene_description: She’s about to get in when…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What the hell was that?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just wanted to see what all the fuss was about.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’ve had better.

scenes.scene_description: Suddenly, Phoebe leans in and kisses her on the lips!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! I found one that fits!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y’know what they say, the 23 rd time’s the charm. Aww, look at you all handsome!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whose is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh does it matter?! All that matters is that you look so handsome.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whose is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t want to say.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on! I don’t care! Come on! Whose is it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Diane Keeton.

scenes.scene_description: Ralph Lauren , Rachel is hooking Chandler up with another tuxedo.

title: The One With The Vows

lines.speaker: Episodes Originally Transcribed by
lines.text: Eric Aasen , Mindy Mattingly Phillips , and guineapig .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you realize that four weeks from today we’re getting married? Four weeks baby!! Four weeks!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you realize you get louder each week?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There’s still so much to do. Have you written your vows yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I figured I’d buy those. Pat, I’d like to buy a vow.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweetie, you know I have no sense of humor when it comes to the wedding.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right. So uh, have you written yours yet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! But I know exactly what I’m going to say.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you happen to know what I’m going to say?
